entries.title: Android Canary  |  Platform  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the Android Canary program, which allows developers to test pre-release Android builds. The changes involve updates to the title, addition of navigation links, and minor formatting adjustments.  These changes enhance the document's structure and readability.

entries.title: Distribution dashboard  |  Platform  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document's title and source were updated, and introductory text was revised for clarity and to include navigation links. Formatting was also updated for better readability and consistency.

entries.title: Android Releases  |  Platform  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document's title and URL were updated, and additional links to other Android developer resources were added. Also, a section of older Android releases was added with summaries.

entries.title: Android 10  |  Platform  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document has been updated to include a navigation menu, organize content using a new structure with headers such as "Privacy features", "Behavior changes", and "New features & APIs" that includes links to relevant pages. Also, added and updated the steps required to prepare apps for Android 10.

entries.title: Android 10 features and APIs  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the features and API changes introduced in Android 10, covering security enhancements, connectivity features, telephony improvements, changes to external storage, and media/graphics updates. It provides links to relevant documentation for developers.

entries.title: Get Android 10  |  Platform  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for getting Android 10 has been updated to reflect the current links, formatting, and instructions.  It includes updated instructions for using the Android Emulator, and removes redundant information.

entries.title: Android 10 for Developers  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Android 10 highlights several updates, including improvements to UI, user privacy, and new APIs for features like foldable devices, dark themes, and gesture navigation. The changes include adding links, updating image paths, and minor text adjustments for clarity and consistency.

entries.title: Privacy in Android 10  |  Platform  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the privacy changes in Android 10. The changes include updates to scoped storage, more user control over location permissions, restrictions on background activity starts, and restrictions on hardware identifiers, as well as permission requirements for wireless scanning. These changes impact how apps access user data and interact with the system.

entries.title: Privacy changes in Android 10  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details privacy-focused changes in Android 10, including updates to external storage access, background location permission, restrictions on background activity starts, removal of contacts affinity, MAC address randomization, and restrictions on accessing the /proc/net filesystem. These changes enhance user privacy and control over data access.

entries.title: Set up the Android 10 SDK  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides instructions on setting up the Android 10 SDK within Android Studio. It includes updated links, formatting adjustments, and clarifies the necessary steps for SDK installation and build configuration, ensuring developers have the correct tools to develop and test applications for Android 10.

entries.title: Android 11  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Android 11 has been updated with several changes, including modifications to headings, the addition of introductory links to Android Developers resources, and the updating of URLs and formatting. These changes aim to improve the document's structure, provide better navigation, and enhance the overall user experience.

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 11  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes behavior changes for apps targeting Android 11. Key changes include enhanced privacy with scoped storage, permission resets, background location restrictions, and package visibility filtering. Security updates involve native heap pointer tagging and toast restrictions. Connectivity updates include APN database access restrictions. Accessibility service changes affect runtime declarations, and media intent actions now require the system default camera.

entries.title: Behavior changes: all apps  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes behavior changes in Android 11 that affect all apps, regardless of their target SDK version. Key changes include enhancements to user privacy with one-time permissions and restrictions on SIM identifier access, updates to exposure notifications, improvements to SSL socket implementations, and adjustments to how app usage statistics are handled.  Additionally, there are changes related to emulator support for 5G, JobScheduler API call limits debugging, file descriptor sanitization, and non-SDK interface restrictions.  The Maps v1 shared library has been removed. Furthermore, when sharing content URIs, the intent must grant URI access permissions.

entries.title: Features and APIs Overview  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of features and APIs introduced in Android 11. It includes updates to device controls, media controls, screen support, conversations, privacy, security, and secure data sharing. The changes are designed to enhance user experience, improve privacy, and provide developers with more control and flexibility.

entries.title: Captive portal API support  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the captive portal API support in Android 11. The changes include updates to the title and URL, minor text adjustments, and the addition of screenshots with captions. It also highlights the support for `venue-info-url` and potential future use cases for session time and data caps.

entries.title: Updates to non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 11  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides updates on non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 11. It highlights changes in blocked interfaces, including those transitioning from greylisted to blocked status and those newly added to the SDK. The document also provides information on how to test and debug these changes using ADB commands.

entries.title: Data access auditing  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for data access auditing in Android 11 has been updated. The changes include updates to the title, URL, and source of the document. Additionally, it now provides links to Android Developers, Essentials, and Releases, and updates the link to the data access auditing guide.

entries.title: Foreground services in Android 11  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes changes to foreground service behavior in Android 11, specifically regarding access to location, camera, and microphone. It highlights restrictions on accessing these resources when a foreground service is running in the background, and mandates the use of specific foreground service types for camera and microphone access. The document also provides links to related documentation.

entries.title: Location updates in Android 11  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes changes to location access in Android 11. Key updates include one-time location access, a more restrictive approach to background location permissions, and how these changes affect apps. This matters because it impacts how apps request and receive location data, enhancing user privacy.

entries.title: Storage updates in Android 11  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides updates on storage changes in Android 11. It covers enhancements to scoped storage, restrictions on accessing other apps' data, and changes to permissions related to file access. These changes aim to improve user privacy and data security.

entries.title: Set up the Android 11 SDK  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides instructions for setting up the Android 11 SDK in Android Studio. The changes include updating the title, adding a new section with links, and minor text refinements for clarity. These changes help developers to easily find the relevant information to set up the SDK and get started with Android 11 development.

entries.title: Android 12  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Android 12 has been updated with changes to the links and formatting. This includes updating the URLs to be more consistent and moving around of text. It also includes new links to other developer resources.

entries.title: 12L feature drop  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the 12L feature drop for Android, focusing on improvements for tablets and foldable devices. Key changes include UI optimization for large screens, enhanced multitasking with a new taskbar, and improved compatibility for apps. The document provides links to resources for developers to optimize their apps for these devices.

entries.title: Get 12L  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to formatting, navigation, and links. Specifically, the title and URL have been updated, image paths have been changed, and internal and external links were updated for improved navigation and accuracy.

entries.title: Set up the 12L SDK  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document was updated to reflect the new Android 12L SDK setup process, including updated instructions for installing the SDK within Android Studio, and includes an updated link for the Android Studio download.

entries.title: Behavior changes: all apps  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines behavior changes in Android 12 that affect all apps, regardless of their target SDK. Key changes include updates to overscroll effects, splash screen implementation, web intent resolution, immersive mode behavior, deprecation of `Display.getRealSize()` and `Display.getRealMetrics()`, and multi-window mode enhancements. These changes aim to improve user experience, enhance app compatibility, and adapt to different device form factors. Developers should test and modify their apps to ensure proper functionality and adherence to the new behaviors.  Additionally, there are changes related to how camera apps handle screen orientation and aspect ratios on foldable devices and in multi-window modes.

entries.title: Deprecations  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details deprecations in Android 12. It highlights the deprecation of RenderScript, Android playlists, and methods for retrieving display size information. These changes encourage developers to adopt newer APIs for improved performance, compatibility, and screen size handling, and the document explains why these APIs are being deprecated and suggests alternatives.

entries.title: Developer Support images for Pixel  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update modifies the documentation related to Developer Support images for Pixel devices. It includes updated links, formatting changes, and minor content adjustments for clarity and accuracy.  It also includes the removal of the download button and incorporates the direct download link.

entries.title: Features and APIs Overview  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces updates to the Android 12 features documentation. The changes include updates to links, formatting, and the addition of features like AppSearch, Game Mode, and Picture-in-picture (PiP) improvements.

entries.title: Android 12 widgets improvements  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the Android 12 widget improvements, including rounded corners, device theming, personalization options, new compound buttons, flexible layouts, widget picker enhancements, smoother transitions, simplified RemoteViews collections, and runtime modification of RemoteViews.

entries.title: Migrate apps to Android 12  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on migrating Android apps to Android 12, covering compatibility testing, updating targeting, and utilizing new APIs. The changes include updates to the document's title and URL, as well as minor wording adjustments and markdown formatting improvements throughout the guide.

entries.title: Updates to non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 12  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details updates to non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 12. It explains which previously greylisted interfaces are now blocked and which new interfaces have been added to the Android SDK. It also provides guidance on how to identify and address the use of non-SDK interfaces in your apps.

entries.title: Android 12 features and changes list  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines the features and behavior changes in Android 12, categorized for developers. The primary change is the updating of the title and URL.

entries.title: Android 13  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Android 13 has been updated. The changes include adding a link to the Android Developers main page, updating links to HTTPS, and improving the formatting and presentation of the content for better readability. These changes are important for developers to stay up-to-date with the latest Android version and its features and resources.

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 13 or higher  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details behavior changes for apps targeting Android 13. Key changes include new runtime permissions for Wi-Fi devices, granular media permissions, background body sensor permission requirements, and adjustments to media controls and battery resource utilization. These changes affect how apps request permissions, manage media files, and interact with system resources, impacting user privacy and app functionality.

entries.title: Features and APIs Overview  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes new features and APIs introduced in Android 13. Key changes include improvements to developer productivity, theming, per-app language preferences, text handling, and system font rendering. These enhancements offer developers new tools to improve app user experience, especially in areas like internationalization and UI customization. The changes require developers to adapt to new APIs and potentially adjust their UI to align with improved text rendering and theming capabilities.

entries.title: Tablet and large screen support  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of Android 13's enhancements for large screens, including improvements to the system UI, multitasking, compatibility mode, and palm rejection. It also highlights changes to Google Play to promote apps optimized for large screens and provides resources for developers to test and optimize their apps for these devices. Several links were updated, and formatting/style changes were made.

entries.title: Get Android 13  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for getting Android 13 has been updated. Changes include updating the title and links, reformatting of the lists, improvements in the instructions for setting up emulators, and better image and video embedding. These changes aim to improve the clarity and usability of the documentation for developers.

entries.title: Set up the Android 13 SDK  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides instructions on setting up the Android 13 SDK in Android Studio. The changes include updating the SDK installation steps, providing updated code examples for build configuration in Groovy and Kotlin, and referencing relevant documentation for behavior changes and new features.

entries.title: Android 13 features and changes list  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines the features and changes introduced in Android 13, categorized by functionality such as Camera, Connectivity, and Privacy. It provides a comprehensive list for developers to understand the impact on their applications, including new APIs, behavior changes, and updates to existing features.

entries.title: Android 14  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Android 14 has been updated, with changes including updated links, formatting adjustments, and the use of HTTPS for URLs. These changes improve the document's presentation and ensure the links are secure and functional, enhancing the user experience.

entries.title: Behavior changes: all apps  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes behavior changes in Android 14 that affect all apps, regardless of their target SDK. Key changes include modifications to exact alarm permissions, context-registered broadcast queuing, app process termination, Bluetooth MTU handling, app standby bucket restrictions, mlock limits, system resource usage enforcement for cached apps, and the behavior of non-dismissable foreground notifications. These changes aim to improve system stability, performance, and user experience.

entries.title: Data safety information is more visible  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android 14 enhances data safety visibility by displaying declared data sharing information in more system locations, particularly within permission dialogs and a new location data sharing updates page accessible through system settings. This change aims to increase user awareness and control over their data sharing practices.

entries.title: Foreground service types are required  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the changes in foreground service types required for Android 14. The update emphasizes the necessity of specifying foreground service types in the manifest and at runtime, provides guidance on declaring the appropriate permissions, and highlights the importance of adhering to runtime requirements for each service type. Several new types have been introduced and the document also provides examples and cautions for developers.

entries.title: Updates to non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 14  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides updates on non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 14. The changes include an updated list of restricted non-SDK interfaces, emphasizing the potential risks of using non-SDK methods and fields. It advises developers to test their apps for non-SDK interface usage and provides guidance on migration to SDK alternatives or requesting new public APIs if necessary.

entries.title: Schedule exact alarms are denied by default  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details changes to how exact alarms are scheduled in Android 14. Specifically, the `SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM` permission, introduced in Android 12, is now denied by default for newly installed apps targeting Android 13 and higher. The document explains the implications of this change, including which apps are affected, how to request the permission, and alternative approaches for scheduling tasks.

entries.title: Schema for app metadata bundles  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the schema for app metadata bundles in Android 14. These bundles are used to provide information about an app, such as data safety practices. The main changes include clarifying how device manufacturers and app installers provide metadata, updating the format and structure of various data fields, and providing more details on specific keys and values within the schema.

entries.title: App metadata bundles for data safety  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces and explains the app metadata bundles for data safety feature, outlining what developers need to disclose regarding their app's data collection and sharing practices. Key changes include updated links, clearer explanations of data collection and sharing, and clarifications on exemptions like ephemeral processing and end-to-end encryption.

entries.title: Add support for built-in and custom predictive back animations  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces updates to the predictive back feature in Android, including the addition of support for custom in-app transitions and animations.  It provides guidance on how to integrate these features using AndroidX APIs and platform APIs, alongside code examples and XML, focusing on the use of TransitionManager and OnBackPressedCallback for custom animations triggered by the predictive back gesture. It also includes links to resources and examples.

entries.title: Large screen user per-app overrides  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces user-per-app overrides for large screens in Android 14 QPR1. It explains how users can change the aspect ratio of apps through device settings and how developers can disable or modify this behavior using `PackageManager.Property` tags within the app manifest. It provides code samples for disabling the overrides and encourages developers to optimize apps for large screens using window size classes.

entries.title: Get Android 14  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides updated instructions for obtaining Android 14, including information on Google Pixel devices, the Android Emulator, and Generic System Images (GSIs). It also includes updated links and instructions for setting up and using these resources for testing and development.

entries.title: Set up the Android 14 SDK  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides instructions on setting up the Android 14 SDK in Android Studio, including installing the SDK packages and updating the app's build configuration. It also provides links to relevant documentation about behavior changes and new features in Android 14. These changes allow developers to use the Android 14 APIs and test their apps for compatibility.

entries.title: Android 15  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The changes in this document primarily involve updating links, using HTTPS, and adjusting formatting for consistency. The document also incorporates minor rewording for clarity. These updates enhance the document's presentation and usability.

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 15 or higher
entries.summary: The documentation updates the links to the Material 3 and Material 2 Compose components to point to the correct composable functions.  This change ensures that developers are directed to the appropriate documentation for these components, improving the accuracy and usability of the documentation.

entries.title: Behavior changes: all apps  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines behavior changes in Android 15 that affect all apps. Key changes include modifications to how the system handles the package stopped state, introduces support for 16KB memory page sizes which may require recompilation for apps using native code, and provides guidance for apps to support the new private space feature, including specific requirements for launcher apps.

entries.title: Changes to foreground service types for Android 15  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines changes to foreground service types in Android 15. The changes include details on `mediaProcessing`, restrictions on launching foreground services from `BOOT_COMPLETED` broadcast receivers, and the behavior of `Service.onTimeout()`.

entries.title: Updates to non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 15  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document, which has been updated for Android 15, outlines restrictions on non-SDK interfaces. It explains that Android 15 includes updated lists of restricted non-SDK interfaces based on collaboration with developers and internal testing. It also advises developers to test their apps for non-SDK interface usage and provides guidance on migrating to SDK alternatives or requesting a new public API if necessary. The changes include updates to the document's title and URL references.

entries.title: Features and APIs Overview  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the key features and APIs in Android 15. The changes include enhancements to camera and media functionalities, developer productivity tools, and improvements to PDF rendering capabilities. These updates provide developers with new tools and features to enhance app functionality and user experience.

entries.title: Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides feedback channels for Android 15 QPR2, including the issue tracker and Android Beta Feedback app. The changes include updating the title, adding quick links, and modifying image sources and links for consistency and improved user experience. The document also provides guidelines on how to report and track issues effectively, emphasizing the importance of checking release notes and existing issues before submitting new ones.

entries.title: Release notes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the release notes for Android 15 QPR2 Beta 3, including updates to supported devices, bug fixes, and known issues. Key changes include updated links, improvements to the presentation of information, and the addition of Pixel 9 devices to the list of supported devices for the 16KB developer option.

entries.title: Behavior changes: all apps  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines behavior changes introduced in Android 16. Key changes include JobScheduler quota optimizations affecting background task execution, the deprecation of JobInfo.setImportantWhileForeground, stricter enforcement of broadcast priority scopes, and enhancements for compatibility with 16KB memory pages. These changes aim to improve system performance, background task management, and app compatibility.  Developers should test their apps and make necessary adjustments to ensure compatibility and optimal performance.

entries.title: Updates to non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 16  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document, which has been updated for Android 16, provides guidance on restricted non-SDK interfaces. It emphasizes that apps using non-SDK interfaces face a risk of breakage, encourages testing to identify such usage, and suggests requesting new public APIs when SDK alternatives are unavailable.

entries.title: OTA images for Google Pixel  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides OTA images for Google Pixel devices. The primary change involves updating the document title, source, and content to reflect the latest Android 16 QPR2 builds and adjust the download links with checksums. The format for displaying the download links has also been updated, replacing buttons with direct text links.

entries.title: OTA images for Google Pixel  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides OTA (Over-The-Air) images for Google Pixel devices. The changes include updates to the device list, link updates, and formatting adjustments. Additionally, a new build date and download links for Android 16 beta images for various Pixel devices were added. The inclusion of new Pixel devices and the addition of device-specific download links enable developers and users to test and update their devices with the latest Android 16 beta builds.

entries.title: Features and APIs
entries.summary: The code example for drawing vertically-oriented text in Compose has been updated. The change replaces an older lambda syntax `drawIntoCanvas { canvas ->` with the more concise `drawIntoCanvas >{ canvas -`.  This simplification streamlines the code's visual clarity without altering its functionality.

entries.title: Progress-centric notifications  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces updates to the progress-centric notifications documentation, including changes to the title and URL, along with updates to code snippets, and formatting for improved clarity and readability. It provides best practices for developers to create effective progress-centric notifications.

entries.title: Get Android 16 QPR beta builds  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document was updated to reflect the latest information regarding obtaining Android 16 QPR beta builds. This includes updated links, improved formatting, and clarifications on device compatibility and enrollment in the Android Beta program.  The specific Pixel devices supported were updated as well.

entries.title: Get Android 16  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides updated instructions for obtaining Android 16, including updates to links, formatting, and the addition of a resizable emulator image. It also adds a few links to the top of the document for easier navigation.

entries.title: Android 16 GSI binaries and release notes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides release notes and download links for Android 16 GSI binaries. The updates include refreshed device lists, and general advisory clarifications. The download section and license agreement have also been updated.

entries.title: Android 16 GSI binaries and release notes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides release notes for Android 16 GSI binaries, which are intended for developers to test and validate their apps.  It includes information on device requirements, known issues, and download links, along with the terms and conditions for using the GSI binaries.

entries.title: Android 16 QPR2  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document was updated to reflect the current state of Android 16 QPR2 Beta, including updated links and formatting. It also streamlined the structure and improved readability.

entries.title: OTA images for Google Pixel  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides OTA (Over-The-Air) update images for Google Pixel devices to recover from an Android 16 Beta build. It includes a list of supported devices, instructions for applying the OTA images, and a warning about potential data loss during the process. The file was updated to reflect current device model names, download links and checksums.

entries.title: Get Android 16 QPR2  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides instructions on how to obtain Android 16 QPR2, including options for Google Pixel devices, the Android Emulator, and Generic System Images (GSI). The update includes improvements to the user interface, updated images, and a new resizable emulator feature. The "title" and "url" were updated and several links were updated to reflect the new release.

entries.title: Android 16 QPR2 GSI binaries and release notes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides release notes for Android 16 QPR2 GSI binaries, detailing changes, known issues, and download links for developers to test and validate their apps. The changes include updated device lists, improved formatting, and minor wording adjustments.

entries.title: Android Beta for developers  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of the Android Beta program. Changes include updating the title and URL, reformatting the bullet points, and clarifying information about milestones, SDK tools, and feedback channels. These changes enhance the clarity and organization of the documentation.

entries.title: Release notes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update includes improvements to the release notes, including updated links and formatting, as well as the addition of the known issues that were addressed in Beta 3. The API diff links now use relative paths, and there are updates to the device lists and support information.

entries.title: Set up the Android 16 QPR2 SDK  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines the steps for setting up the Android 16 QPR2 SDK in Android Studio. The changes include updates to the title, introduction of breadcrumb links, and some formatting adjustments for better readability. It also provides instructions to update the app's build configuration with the correct compile SDK version, as well as the steps on how to install the Android 16 QPR2 SDK.

entries.title: OTA images for Google Pixel  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides OTA images for Google Pixel devices. The changes include updates to the document title, source, and formatting, including removal of HTML artifacts, and updates to the device list. The device list has been updated to include the upcoming Pixel 9, 9 Pro, 9 Pro XL, and 9 Pro Fold along with the Pixel 9a and Pixel 10 devices. The formatting of the download table has been updated to be more concise.

entries.title: Factory images for Google Pixel  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides factory images for Google Pixel devices. It lists the available builds, download links, and checksums for different Pixel models, enabling users to flash the images manually or using the Android Flash Tool.

entries.title: Release notes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the changes in Android 16 QPR3 Beta 2, which includes fixes for app drawer responsiveness, Android Auto battery drain, notification shade glitches, excessive battery drain, charging limit issues, slow Wi-Fi speeds, and other system stability issues. These updates improve the user experience and address critical bugs.

entries.title: Release notes (8/14/2025)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the release notes for Android 16 QPR1 Beta 3.1 released on August 14, 2025. It includes fixes for various issues, such as device instability related to Wi-Fi and Context Hub, home screen and notification display problems, unexpected device reboots, and issues with Bluetooth and media playback. The updates aim to improve system stability and user experience.

entries.title: Release notes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update to the Android 16 release notes includes improvements to the formatting, updated links, and addresses several bug fixes related to haptic performance, video recording, display issues, and app crashes. The changes also include clarifications on CTS approval and Play Integrity API.

entries.title: Set up the Android 16 SDK  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides instructions for setting up the Android 16 SDK, including updating build configurations and installing the SDK within Android Studio. It also links to resources detailing behavior changes and new features in Android 16. The most notable change is the conversion of some markdown formatting and links to match the new markdown standard.

entries.title: Android 16 features and changes list  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines the features and changes introduced in Android 16, covering various aspects like core functionality, user interface, security, and connectivity. It details behavior changes for all apps and those targeting Android 16, along with new APIs.

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 17 or higher
entries.summary: The document has been updated to reflect that apps targeting Android 17 need to change the `targetSDKVersion` to API Level 37. This is necessary to comply with the new local network permission requirements.

entries.title: Behavior changes: all apps  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines behavior changes for all apps on Android 17. Key changes include security enhancements like the deprecation plan for `usesCleartextTraffic`, improvements to key limits in Android Keystore, user experience changes related to IME visibility after rotation, touchpad behavior during pointer capture, and restrictions on background audio interactions.

entries.title: Restrictions on orientation and resizability are ignored  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes changes in Android 17 (API level 37) where orientation, resizability, and aspect ratio restrictions are ignored on displays with a smallest width greater than 600dp.  This change removes the temporary developer opt-out from Android 16 and encourages developers to make their apps adaptive to various screen sizes and orientations to deliver the best user experience.

entries.title: MessageQueue behavior change guidance  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes a significant change in Android 17 (API level 37) regarding the `MessageQueue` implementation. The change introduces a lock-free implementation to improve performance, potentially affecting apps that rely on runtime reflection to access internal details of the `MessageQueue`. The document provides guidance on mitigating potential issues and offers testing strategies to ensure compatibility.

entries.title: OTA images for Google Pixel
entries.summary: The documentation for downloading OTA images has been updated to clarify the enrollment process for beta builds. Instead of generic "Developer Preview" builds, the documentation now correctly describes the enrollment in the Android Beta for Pixel program, which provides OTA updates.

entries.title: Factory images for Google Pixel  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation updates the title and source, introduces new links, and clarifies the Android beta program. Additionally, it removes the interactive download buttons and presents the download links and checksums in a more accessible table format.

entries.title: Features and APIs  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces new features and APIs in Android 17. Key updates include enhancements to core functionality like profiling and job debugging, improvements in privacy with the Android Contacts Picker, security features like Android Advanced Protection Mode and hybrid post-quantum cryptography, connectivity improvements for satellite networks, user experience enhancements like a dedicated Assistant volume stream and Handoff, and Live Update with Semantic Coloring APIs.

entries.title: Contact picker  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the Contact Picker feature, a standardized interface for users to share contact information with apps. The changes include code examples for launching the picker in single and multi-select modes, and a note about handling the results with a `ContentResolver`.

entries.title: Get Android 17  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the Beta program for Android 17, providing instructions on how to get the Beta on a Google Pixel device, and adding information on Generic System Images (GSI).

entries.title: Android 17 GSI binaries and release notes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the Android 17 GSI binaries, which are available for developers to test and validate their apps. It provides download links, device requirements, flashing instructions, known issues, and the terms and conditions for using the GSI. The document also includes updates to the Pixel device list, and a new format for the download section.

entries.title: Migrate apps to Android 17  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on migrating Android apps to Android 17. It covers ensuring app compatibility, updating the app's targeting, and building with new APIs. entries.title: Release notes
entries.summary: This update introduces a section detailing the top issues fixed in Android 17 Beta 3. These fixes address critical system-level regressions and stability issues, including app restarts, screen flickering, camera failures, UI glitches, device freezes, reboots, and issues related to Android Auto, Bluetooth, and battery saver functionality.  Addressing these issues improves the user experience by enhancing stability, resolving UI artifacts, and ensuring the proper functionality of core system features.

These changes aim to enhance user experience and provide developers with tools to build more engaging and efficient applications.

entries.title: Android 6.0 Changes  |  Android Developers

These include a new runtime permissions model, power saving optimizations with Doze and App Standby, removal of the Apache HTTP client, and enhancements to security with BoringSSL and changes to hardware identifier access. It also includes changes to notifications, AudioManager, text selection, browser bookmark handling, Android Keystore, and Wi-Fi/networking APIs.  These changes require developers to update their apps to ensure compatibility and leverage new features while maintaining user privacy and system efficiency.

entries.title: Android 7.0 Behavior Changes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android 7.0 introduces behavior changes impacting battery and memory usage, permissions, file sharing, and accessibility. These changes aim to improve system performance and user experience, requiring developers to adapt their apps to maintain compatibility.

entries.title: Android 7.0 Behavior Changes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android 7.0 enhances Doze mode and optimizes background processes to conserve battery and reduce RAM usage. Apps targeting Android 7.0 and higher are restricted from receiving certain implicit broadcasts, such as `CONNECTIVITY_ACTION`, unless registered with `registerReceiver()`. Also, the system no longer sends `ACTION_NEW_PICTURE` or `ACTION_NEW_VIDEO` broadcasts. These changes require developers to use alternative mechanisms like `JobScheduler` for network operations.

entries.title: Android 7.0 Behavior Changes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: To enhance security, Android 7.0 restricts access to private app directories, triggering `SecurityException` if apps attempt to modify permissions using `MODE_WORLD_READABLE` or `MODE_WORLD_WRITEABLE`. Furthermore, `file://` URIs are prohibited from being exposed outside the app's domain, and the `DownloadManager` can no longer share private files by filename, throwing `FileUriExposedException` and `SecurityException` respectively. Developers must use `FileProvider` and `ContentResolver.openFileDescriptor()` for secure file sharing.

entries.title: Android 7.0 Behavior Changes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Apps targeting Android 7.0 must use `content://` URIs and grant temporary access permissions when sharing files between apps, enforced by the `StrictMode` API. Use of `FileProvider` is recommended to facilitate secure file sharing, as `file://` URIs are no longer allowed.

entries.title: Android 7.0 Behavior Changes  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android 7.0 enhances accessibility, particularly for users with low vision, by introducing screen zoom. Users can adjust the display size to magnify or shrink screen elements, improving usability. Apps should ensure compatibility with this feature.

The document provides guidance on migrating from the kapt annotation processing tool to KSP (Kotlin Symbol Processing) to improve build performance, and steps to make the migration.

entries.title: Enable multidex for apps with over 64K methods  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes how to enable multidex support for Android apps that exceed the 64K method limit.  The changes include minor text updates, code formatting, and updated links. The primary change is the addition of links to Android developers and removal of a link to an external site. There are also examples of Groovy and Kotlin code blocks with appropriate modifications.

entries.title: Native dependencies with the Android Gradle plugin  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on using native dependencies with the Android Gradle plugin. The changes include updates to the title and related links, clarifying the use of prefab feature, and providing example code snippets for CMake configuration. The document highlights the importance of the `prefab` feature and how to configure it to include native dependencies in your project.

entries.title: Optimize your build speed  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the latest recommendations for optimizing Android build speeds. Key changes include updated links, improvements to build configuration suggestions, and additional notes on JVM arguments and heap size configuration to further improve build performance.

entries.title: Profile your build  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on profiling Android builds for performance optimization. It covers the use of the `gradle-profiler` tool for detailed analysis and the Gradle `--profile` option for a command-line approach, along with examples of how to benchmark different build scenarios like incremental builds and clean builds.

entries.title: Publish your library  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation's title and URL were updated to reflect a more specific and accurate description of the content. Also, the document was updated to use a consistent Markdown format. The links were updated to their relative paths.

entries.title: Configure publication variants  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes how to configure publication variants in Android library projects. It provides guidance on publishing different build variants with their own attributes, using Gradle Module Metadata (GMM), and configuring the publication using single or multiple variant approaches. The changes include URL updates, content restructuring, and improved clarity in explaining concepts like build variants, artifacts, and attributes, and links to relevant documentation.

entries.title: Prepare your library for release  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes how to prepare an Android library project for release. It covers essential configurations like setting the namespace, choosing the minimum SDK version, setting up AAR metadata, and enabling test fixtures.  These steps are crucial for ensuring your library integrates correctly with other projects.

entries.title: Upload your library  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document, which guides developers on publishing Android libraries, has been updated. The changes include updates to the title, introduction of a navigation section, and slight rewording to improve clarity.

entries.title: Configure how R8 runs  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document, which explains how to configure R8 execution profiles to improve build performance, has been updated. The changes include updates to the document title, links, and code snippets, improving the clarity and formatting of the content. This update refines the guidance for optimizing Android build processes.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release introduces several improvements. It enhances unit testing with new features like local JVM testing, new test tasks, and configurations for test-only dependencies. It also updates dependency management, resolves issues with scopes, and improves resource merging and project evaluation speeds.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release of the Android plugin for Gradle (1.1.2) includes updates to fix issues related to unit testing, build configurations, and manifest merging. Specifically, it normalizes paths for mockable JAR creation, corrects the 'archivesBaseName' setting, and resolves placeholder failures in the manifest merger for library test applications. These changes improve build stability and reliability.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release of the Android Gradle plugin (AGP) version 1.3.0 introduces several enhancements. Key improvements include the ability to control the task thread pool size, exclusion of license files by default, improved unit test support for multi-flavor and build variant source folders, support for command-line arguments for instrumentation tests, and the ability to add custom parameters for AAPT. Additionally, a test APK module is supported, resource name validation has been added, and changes have been made to the manifest merger settings for AAR packages.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Android plugin for Gradle, revision 1.3.1. It includes fixes related to ZipAlign, Renderscript packaging with NDK, the createDebugCoverageReport task, support for the archiveBaseName property, and the Invalid ResourceType lint warning.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release of the Android plugin for Gradle (AGP) introduces the Transform API for manipulating compiled class files, deprecates the dex getter, improves MultiDex support, adds features for ABI configuration and vector drawable generation, and includes various bug fixes and performance improvements. These changes enhance build flexibility, compatibility, and efficiency.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release of the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) introduces Instant Run, incremental build support, and improvements to build speed and resource shrinking. It also adds new signing options and fixes a duplicate dependency issue.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update to the Android Gradle Plugin 2.1.0 release notes includes dependency updates, support for the N Developer Preview, and improvements to build performance. Key changes include support for Java 8 features using the Jack toolchain, default incremental Java compilation, and dexing-in-process for faster builds. These changes improve development efficiency and enable the use of new language features.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release of the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) version 2.2.0 introduces several enhancements, including support for CMake and ndk-build for native libraries, auto-download of missing SDK components, an experimental build cache, and APK signing with v2 signature scheme. It also includes improvements to the Jack compiler and multidex build configuration. These updates aim to improve build performance, provide more flexibility in native development, and enhance the overall developer experience.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines the release notes for Android Gradle Plugin 3.4.0, including new features, behavior changes, and known issues. Key changes involve new lint check dependency configurations, deprecation warnings, enabling R8 by default, and deprecation of ndkCompile. It also includes updates for compatibility with Android 11 package visibility.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides release notes for Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 3.5.0, detailing changes, improvements, and known issues. The changes include updates to links and formatting, minor version updates, and the introduction of features like incremental annotation processing and cacheable unit tests. The document also addresses compatibility with package visibility in Android 11 and provides guidance on resolving potential warnings related to the Kotlin Gradle plugin.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Android Gradle Plugin 3.6.0. Key changes include View Binding, support for Maven Publish plugin, a new default packaging tool (zipflinger), native build attribution, and several behavior changes related to native libraries, R class sharing, resource handling, and annotation processing.  It also includes bug fixes and known issues.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 4.0.0. Key changes include updates for Android 11 package visibility, Build Analyzer enhancements, Java 8 library desugaring in D8 and R8, and the introduction of buildFeatures to optimize build performance.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release of the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 4.1.0 includes improvements to Kotlin DSL and API, enabling exporting native libraries in AAR for Android Library projects, R8 support for Kotlin metadata, and behavior changes like removing the build cache and renaming properties. It also addresses issues related to unit tests in libraries and deprecates the io.fabric Gradle plugin.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 4.2.0. Key changes include defaulting to Java 8, a new JVM resource compiler, the ability to configure app signing per variant, and improvements to native build output. Additionally, there are changes related to Gradle compatibility and DEX file compression.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the release notes for Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 7.0.0 and its subsequent minor update 7.0.1, covering new features, improvements, and breaking changes. Key changes include updates to compatibility requirements, a stable Variant API, enhanced lint tasks, improved R8 handling of missing classes, and the removal of several dependency configurations. Additionally, the document highlights known issues and provides workarounds.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release includes improvements to C/C++ module referencing, lint configuration, and Maven publishing. Specifically, it enables C/C++ modules to reference each other within a project, ensures lint configurations respect order, and updates the handling of automatic component creation for Maven publishing. These changes improve build flexibility, configuration control, and compatibility with other libraries.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release notes document describes the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 7.2.0, along with subsequent minor updates (7.2.1 and 7.2.2). It details bug fixes, compatibility requirements, and new features like test fixtures. It's important for developers to stay updated to ensure their projects function correctly.

entries.title: Android Gradle Plugin 8.0.0 (April 2023)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release note for Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 8.0.0 provides a summary of changes, including compatibility requirements, breaking changes, and new features. Key improvements include enhanced build performance and a new settings plugin for centralizing configurations and managing tool execution profiles.  AGP 8.0 requires Gradle 8.0, JDK 17, and includes breaking changes regarding namespace configuration, and default values for build flags.

entries.title: Android Gradle Plugin 8.12.0 (July 2025)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release note documents the changes in Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 8.12.0. It includes updates to the minimum and default versions of Gradle, SDK Build Tools, NDK, and JDK. It also lists fixed issues in AGP versions 8.12.2, 8.12.1 and 8.12.0, categorized by the area they affect, such as AGP or Lint, and linking to the related issue trackers.

entries.title: Android Gradle Plugin 8.2.0 (Nov 2023)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines the release notes for Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 8.2.0, including compatibility information, a new macro for specifying the JDK path, and lists of fixed issues in AGP 8.2.0, 8.2.1 and 8.2.2. The new macro simplifies JDK path configuration for Gradle daemons, enhancing build safety and reducing errors. Several issues related to the Android Gradle Plugin, Dexer (D8), and Shrinker (R8) have been resolved in these releases.

entries.title: Android Gradle Plugin 8.3.0 (Feb 2024)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release adds support for Gradle Version Catalogs with code completion, navigation, and integration with the Project Structure dialog. It also includes new SDK insights regarding Play policy violations and compileSdk version support. Furthermore, it contains fixes in patch releases for AGP and Lint integration.

entries.title: Android Gradle Plugin 8.9.0 (March 2025)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the release notes for Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 8.9.0. It includes compatibility information and lists resolved issues in AGP 8.9.0, 8.9.1, and 8.9.2, covering areas like the Android Gradle Plugin itself, Dexer (D8), Lint, and R8 (Shrinker). These fixes address various build and tooling problems, including resource shrinking, Dexer errors, and linting issues.

entries.title: Android Gradle plugin 9.0.1 (January 2026)  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android Gradle plugin 9.0.1 introduces API and behavior changes, including updates to the DSL and API interfaces, and deprecates some APIs. It also provides guidance on migrating projects to the new interfaces and updating build files, custom build logic, and third-party plugins. Additionally, it removes the ability to opt-out of the new DSL in AGP 10.0 and introduces built-in Kotlin support.

entries.title: Android Gradle plugin API updates  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details API changes in the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP). Key changes include the deprecation and removal of certain APIs like RenderScript, Transform API, and Resource Configurations.  It provides migration guidance and introduces new APIs within the `androidComponents{}` block and the `Artifacts` API for improved build performance and flexibility.  Additionally, there are updates to how `AndroidComponentsExtension` is used, requiring type adaptation for Kotlin Multiplatform projects.

entries.title: Android Gradle Plugin DSL/API migration timeline  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines the planned evolution of the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) DSL and API, detailing deprecations, removals, and the introduction of stable APIs. Key changes include the stabilization of new Variant and DSL APIs, deprecation and removal of old APIs, and the potential removal of access to private internal AGP classes in AGP 9.0 (January 2026). These changes matter because they impact plugin development and require developers to adapt to the new APIs and opt-in mechanisms to maintain compatibility.

These changes help clarify the usage of the Ink API for drawing strokes in Compose.

entries.title: DropHelper for simplified drag and drop  |  Views  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides an update to the DropHelper documentation, detailing how to simplify drag and drop implementations in Android apps using the DropHelper class. The changes include updates to the document title, links and styling, as well as clarifying and expanding on key aspects such as setting drag sources, configuring drop targets, customizing highlighting, handling editable text fields, and managing MIME types, permissions, and content validation. The purpose is to offer more clarity and guidance for developers employing the DropHelper in their Android projects.

entries.title: Overview  |  Views  |  Android Developers

entries.title: Detect common gestures  |  Views  |  Android Developers

It covers using `onTouchEvent()`, `GestureDetector`, `OnTouchListener`, and how to implement them in both Kotlin and Java, including examples of handling touch events, and detecting gestures like double-tap, long press, and fling. The document also details the use of `GestureDetectorCompat` and `GestureDetector.SimpleOnGestureListener` to streamline gesture recognition.

entries.title: Track touch and pointer movements  |  Views  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has been updated with changes including updated links, new formatting, and improved examples for tracking touch and pointer movements in Android. The examples are available in both Kotlin and Java, and it covers calculating velocity and using pointer capture.

entries.title: Handle multi-touch gestures  |  Views  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides comprehensive guidelines on handling multi-touch gestures in Android. It details the system-generated touch events, how to track individual pointers using indices and IDs, and how to retrieve `MotionEvent` actions. The update includes formatting changes, improved code snippets, and updated links and resources.

entries.summary: This document describes how to use touch gestures to drag and scale on-screen objects, using `onTouchEvent()` to intercept touch events. The document has been updated with information on drag and scale, drag to pan, and using touch to perform scaling, with both Kotlin and Java code examples for each.

entries.title: Image keyboard support  |  Views  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces image keyboard support in Android, explaining how IMEs can send rich content like images to apps using the Commit Content API. It details the process for both apps (receiving content) and IMEs (sending content) with code examples and best practices. The changes include URL updates, improved formatting, and minor wording adjustments.

entries.title: Make a classic API request  |  Play Integrity  |  Android Developers

It also includes updated information on how to generate and use nonces for enhanced security, and provides a sequence diagram illustrating the API's high-level design. Additionally, the document offers guidance on retrying requests and includes links to external resources for further information.

The changes include updates to the title and source, clarifications on the attributes, and links to relevant documentation, such as backup and restore, and tagged pointers.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<attribution>` element has been updated. The changes include modifications to the title, source, and description, as well as adjustments to the syntax and attributes sections. These changes refine the explanation of how to use the `<attribution>` tag within the Android manifest for data access auditing and organization. The update provides context and navigation to related resources.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<category>` element has been updated. The title and some links were updated, and the formatting was slightly changed. The core content and descriptions remain the same, so these are minor changes.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the `<compatible-screens>` manifest element, which specifies the screen configurations with which an application is compatible. The changes include updates to the document title, source, and formatting, as well as clarifications and improvements to the descriptions and examples, enhancing readability and accuracy.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the `<data>` element in the Android manifest, which specifies data requirements for intent filters. The changes include updates to the title, source, and links, along with some clarifications and formatting improvements, particularly regarding URI matching and the use of attributes like `scheme`, `host`, `path`, and `mimeType`.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<grant-uri-permission>` element has been updated with changes including updated links, improved formatting, and clarifications in the description and attributes. The changes aim to enhance clarity and readability of the documentation for developers.

entries.title: App manifest overview  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of the Android manifest file. Key changes include updating the title, adding navigation links, and internal documentation links. Changes also include minor adjustments to formatting and code examples.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<meta-data>` element has been updated. The changes include improvements to the description, clarifying the use of `value` and `resource` attributes, and providing a table that shows the data types and the Bundle methods used to retrieve the values.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces changes to the `<permission>` element documentation. The primary changes involve updating the title, adding a new navigation section, clarifying attribute descriptions (especially `android:name` and `android:protectionLevel`), and improving the formatting for better readability.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<permission-group>` element in the Android manifest has been updated. The changes include updates to the title, introduction of navigation links, and minor text refinements to the description and attribute explanations. These revisions improve clarity and align the documentation with current Android developer practices.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<permission-tree>` element has been updated. The title and URL have been modified, and links within the document have been updated to reflect the new Android Developers site structure, including changes to the syntax presentation.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<profileable>` element has been updated. The changes include improvements in formatting, and updates to the description and examples. The updated documentation clarifies the use of the `<profileable>` tag for profiling applications and the associated implications on performance measurement in release builds.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the `<property>` element in the Android manifest, which is used to add custom metadata to application components.  The changes include minor text updates, improved formatting, and clarifications regarding the usage of `android:value` and `android:resource` attributes, including a note about the precedence if both are present.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the `<queries>` element in the Android manifest, which allows apps to specify the other apps they interact with. The changes include reformatting the document, updating links, and providing more details on how to use the element to increase package visibility.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the `<receiver>` element in the Android manifest, which declares a broadcast receiver. The changes include improvements in formatting, and updates to the description and attributes, providing better clarity and more specific guidance, along with some added cross-linking to other documentation pages.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the `<uri-relative-filter-group>` element, which is used to create precise `Intent` matching rules based on URI components like query parameters and fragments. The changes include formatting updates, improved clarity in descriptions, and more comprehensive examples to illustrate how this element functions, especially in relation to the `path*`, `fragment*`, and `query*` attributes.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<uses-feature>` element in the Android manifest has been updated with improved descriptions, formatting, and added cross-references. The changes clarify the purpose and usage of the element in defining hardware and software requirements for an application, and how it is used for filtering on Google Play.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the `<uses-library>` element in the Android manifest, which specifies shared libraries an application requires. Changes include updated links, improved formatting, and minor wording adjustments for clarity.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `<uses-permission>` element has been updated to include a more detailed description of the element's purpose and usage, along with clarifications on how permissions are granted in different Android versions. It also provides more context regarding how Google Play filters applications based on the declared permissions and the use of the `android:maxSdkVersion` attribute.

entries.title: |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the `<uses-sdk>` element in the Android manifest, which specifies the minimum, target, and maximum API levels supported by an application.  The changes include improved clarity, better explanations, and removal of the recommendation to use `maxSdkVersion`.  It explains the implications of setting these attributes on app compatibility and Google Play filtering.

entries.title: Define a custom app permission  |  Privacy  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes how app developers can define custom permissions to share resources and capabilities with other apps. The changes include updating links to the new documentation, improving clarity, and providing better code examples.

entries.title: Permissions on Android  |  Privacy  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Android permissions has been updated. The changes include adding links to other relevant developer resources, improving formatting with bullet points and better images, and clarifying the explanations of different permission types (install-time, signature, runtime, and special permissions). These changes aim to improve the clarity and organization of the information, providing developers with a more user-friendly resource for understanding and implementing permissions in their apps.

entries.title: Content provider basics  |  App data and files  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the new Android Developers website design. Several changes were made including updating the title, adding navigation links, and modifying the formatting of the code examples and tables.

entries.title: Content providers  |  App data and files  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with several changes.  The title, some links, and internal references have been updated. Markdown formatting has also been improved for clarity and consistency.

entries.title: Advanced RenderScript  |  Views  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces advanced concepts in RenderScript, including the runtime and reflected layers. It explains how RenderScript code interacts with the Android framework and how memory is managed, with a focus on functions, variables, and structs within the reflected layer.

entries.title: RenderScript Overview  |  Views  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for RenderScript has been updated to reflect the deprecation of the RenderScript APIs, starting with Android 12. The document now also clarifies the language used to write high-performance compute code and the control API used for managing the lifetime of RenderScript resources. Additionally, the example code snippets have been updated for better clarity and to reflect best practices.

entries.title: Migrate from RenderScript  |  Views  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines how to migrate from RenderScript, which is deprecated in Android 12 and will be removed in a future release. It provides guidance on replacing RenderScript intrinsics, utilizing built-in accelerated image operations like blur and color matrix, and leveraging the Android Graphics Shading Language (AGSL) for custom image processing.  The guide explains how to use RenderEffect for blurring Views or rendering into a Bitmap and using AGSL shaders for custom effects.

entries.title: RenderScript Index  |  Views  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated. The changes include updates to the formatting of the tables and minor updates to the links. It also included additional links to other Android developer documentation pages.

entries.title: Animation resources  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces animation resources in Android. It provides details on property and view animations, including file locations, resource references, and syntax examples for each type of animation.

entries.title: Color state list resource  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has been updated with more precise definitions and examples for the color state list resource. It also includes improvements in formatting, and updates to links and references. The overall structure and attributes of color state lists are clarified.

entries.title: Inline complex XML resources  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation introduces and explains the concept of inline complex XML resources in Android, specifically focusing on animated vector drawables. It details how to define multiple resources within a single XML file using the `<aapt:attr>` tag, offering a more compact approach when resources are not reused elsewhere. This method allows for defining animated vector drawables, vector drawables, and animations within the same XML file, streamlining resource management.

entries.title: Font resources  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for font resources has been updated. The title and source metadata have been modified, and the formatting of code blocks and resource references has been adjusted for improved clarity. Minor changes include added links and text updates throughout the document.

entries.title: Unicode and internationalization support  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides an update to the internationalization support in Android. It mainly updates links, clarifies details on ICU, CLDR, and Unicode versions.  It also updates sections on ICU4J and ICU4C, including versioning and specific API usage, along with Kotlin and Java code examples.

entries.title: Menu resource  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for menu resources has been updated. The changes include updates to the title, adding a description, and minor formatting changes, along with more details to existing attributes, adding clarifications for the use of ProGuard.

entries.title: More resource types  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces additional resource types in Android, including Bool, Color, Dimension, ID, Integer, Integer array, and Typed array, along with their usage and examples. It provides updated formatting and clearer explanations of each resource type, and clarifies how to reference resources in both Java and XML.

entries.title: Test your app with pseudolocales  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has been updated with more details on how to use pseudolocales for testing app localization. The changes include updates to the document title, updated links, improved formatting, and more detailed explanations of the benefits of using pseudolocales for identifying potential localization issues like hardcoded strings, layout problems, string concatenation, and RTL issues.

entries.title: Style resource  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers

The title and URL have been modified, and minor content updates improve clarity and formatting, including code block adjustments and the addition of a 'Next' and 'Previous' link for better navigation. Furthermore, the description has been updated to remove the reference to saving and categorizing content.

entries.title: Make apps more accessible  |  App quality  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides guidelines for developers to improve the accessibility of their Android apps. It covers topics like increasing text visibility, ensuring touch target sizes, and providing content descriptions for UI elements. The document emphasizes the importance of making apps usable for everyone, including people with accessibility needs.

entries.title: Make custom views more accessible  |  App quality  |  Android Developers

It provides a more comprehensive guide by explaining how to handle directional controller clicks, implement accessibility API methods, send accessibility events, and populate accessibility events and node info for custom views. Additionally, it clarifies the use of specific methods like `dispatchPopulateAccessibilityEvent()`, `onInitializeAccessibilityEvent()`, and `onInitializeAccessibilityNodeInfo()`.

entries.title: Health & Fitness Developer Center  |  Android health & fitness  |  Android Developers

entries.title: Community | Health & Fitness Developer Center  |  Android health & fitness  |  Android Developers

entries.title: Read aggregated data  |  Android health & fitness  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document was updated to reflect the deprecation of Google Fit APIs, including a migration guide link. Additionally, code examples were updated to include code block formatting.
