entries.title: Contacts Provider  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces changes to the Contacts Provider documentation, including updates to the title, introduction of navigation links, and modifications to the structure and content related to raw contacts and data organization. The changes provide more context and clarity to the documentation.

entries.title: Manage contact storage locations  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document was updated to reflect changes in the Android Developers documentation. It includes updated links, code formatting, and minor text adjustments for clarity and consistency, ensuring the information remains current and accessible.

entries.title: Credential Manager FAQ  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update modifies the Credential Manager FAQ documentation. It updates the title, adds a navigation bar, and reformats the questions and answers for improved readability. It also refines some of the content and adds emphasis on key benefits and functionalities.

entries.title: Implement Sign in with Google  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides implementation details for Sign in with Google using Credential Manager, outlining steps for adding dependencies, setting up UI flows (bottom sheet and button), handling sign-in responses and errors, and managing sign-out. The changes include code formatting, minor wording adjustments, and updates to the code snippets for clarity. There are also updates to links and notes.

entries.title: About Sign in with Google  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces Sign in with Google using Credential Manager. It provides updated links, clarifies implementation details, and refines the presentation of benefits and prerequisites.

entries.title: Troubleshoot common Credential Manager errors  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the new title and URL for the troubleshooting guide. It also includes new links to Android developer resources and some formatting improvements for the table.

entries.title: Integrate Credential Manager with your credential provider solution  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces how credential providers can integrate with Android's Credential Manager, a set of APIs introduced in Android 14. It outlines the necessary setup steps, including dependency declaration, manifest configuration, and credential type declaration within the provider.  It also explains the two-phased interaction process between Credential Manager and credential providers, involving a begin/query phase and a selection phase, and provides detailed guidance on handling passkey creation requests.

entries.title: Make Credential Manager calls on behalf of other parties for privileged apps  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document explains how privileged apps, like web browsers, can use the Credential Manager on behalf of other parties. It details the use of the `origin` parameter to prevent phishing attacks and how to get approval to handle third-party credentials.

entries.title: Implement Restore Credentials with Credential Manager  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the implementation of Restore Credentials with Credential Manager, including creating, signing in with, and deleting a restore key, along with the necessary prerequisites and dependencies. It also details the steps for creating and handling restore keys, emphasizing the importance of cloud backup and exception handling.

entries.title: About Restore Credentials  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the Restore Credentials feature in Android, which allows users to automatically restore their app accounts and sign in on a new device. The changes include updates to the title and URL, refactoring of links, and minor text edits, improving the readability and structure of the document.

entries.title: Migrate from Smart Lock for Passwords to Credential Manager  |  Identity  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines the migration from Smart Lock for Passwords to Credential Manager, emphasizing the deprecation of Smart Lock and the benefits of using the Credential Manager API. It provides guidance on migration options, including password saving and sign-in, Sign in with Google, and phone number hints, along with code examples for signing in with saved passwords and error handling.

entries.title: Android Mobile App Developer Tools - Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with new URLs, images, and formatting. The update includes more descriptive text and links to relevant resources, and reorganized some of the content.

entries.title: AndroidX overview  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of AndroidX, the successor to the Android Support Library, detailing its features, migration process, and usage in Android projects. Key changes include updating links and descriptions about using the library.

entries.title: Jetpack Compose Roadmap  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The Jetpack Compose roadmap document has been updated. The changes include updates to the document title, links, and table formatting. Specific areas of focus, backlogs, and completed tasks for various core libraries, Jetpack library integrations, and platforms have been revised.

entries.title: Explore the Jetpack libraries by type  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides a categorized listing of Jetpack libraries, now with an updated title and source information. It also removes the category selection buttons and associated formatting.

entries.title: Migrate to AndroidX  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the current state of AndroidX migration. It now emphasizes that most projects already use AndroidX natively and that the `enableJetifier` flag should be used with caution, as it can slow down build times. Additionally, the links have been updated to use the shorter relative paths instead of the full URLs.

entries.title: Artifact Mappings  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for artifact mappings has been updated to reflect changes in the Android developer website. The title and URL have been updated, and navigation links were added.  The CSV download link and version page link were updated to relative links and the table headers now include spaces for better formatting.

entries.title: Class Mappings  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update modifies the file by updating the title, adding navigation links, and correcting internal links. It also changes the location of the CSV download link and ensures the correct formatting of the table.

entries.title: Annotation  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the latest changes in the annotation library. Key updates include the release of version 1.10.0-rc01 and details on changes in the 1.10.0-alpha01 and previous versions, such as dropping support for x86 based Mac and iOS KMP targets, adding the RequiresFlag annotation, and various API changes. It also includes updated links and references.

entries.title: Appcompat  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the appcompat library. The changes include bug fixes and new features, such as the support for per-app locales in non-Activity contexts and the addition of setLineHeight() to TextView compat classes.

entries.title: appfunctions  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details changes to the appfunctions library. The updates include new features such as support for Parcelable, APIs for converting between platform requests and responses, and improvements in the way resources are handled.  Additionally, there are API changes, bug fixes, and improvements to existing functionalities, such as enforcing required fields and adding constraint validations.

entries.title: AppSearch  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the AppSearch documentation, including updates to dependencies, feedback sections, and the introduction of version 1.2 with its alpha release.  Version 1.2 includes API changes, bug fixes and new features such as new built-in types and Action Accumulator API.

entries.title: Arch Core  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the Arch Core library. The main change is that the correct nullability annotations were added to many APIs, which may cause source incompatibility in Kotlin code. Additionally, the maximum number of threads in the ArchExecutor was increased from 2 to 4.

entries.title: AndroidX Release Notes Archive  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document's title and URL were updated, and additional navigation links were added. The anchor links were also updated to use relative paths instead of absolute paths.

entries.title: AndroidX Release Note Archive  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the latest AndroidX releases, including fixes and new features for various components.  Notably, the changes include fixes for issues related to Proguard, Fragment lifecycle, and updates to the PreferenceThemeOverlay, along with fixes in Android Studio related to AndroidX migration and dependency resolution.

entries.title: Architecture Components Release Notes Archive  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document archives the release notes for Architecture Components. The changes include bug fixes, new features, and breaking API changes for WorkManager, Navigation, and Paging components.

entries.title: Autofill  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the Autofill library documentation, including updates to links, dependency declarations, and version release notes. It adds new autofill hint constants and API changes, and modifies the formatting of code snippets and lists.

entries.title: Benchmark  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of the Benchmark library, including its dependencies, setup instructions, and release notes for different versions. The changes include updates to the dependencies, bug fixes, new features, and API changes. These changes improve the library's functionality and address existing issues, especially the updates for baseline profile generation for Kotlin multi-platform modules.

entries.title: Biometric  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the Biometric Jetpack library. The updates include new features such as multiple fallback options support, a new Compose library for easier integration, and API changes for authentication, along with bug fixes and deprecations.

entries.title: bluetooth  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the bluetooth.md file, focusing on the updates related to the AndroidX Bluetooth library. The changes include API modifications, new features, and bug fixes in the 1.0.0-alpha02 and 1.0.0-alpha01 releases.

entries.title: camera featurecombinationquery  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes made to the camera feature combination query library. The changes include updates to the API reference links, dependency declarations, and version release notes, with new features and bug fixes listed.

entries.title: camera media3  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes made to the camera-media3 library. The changes include updates to dependency declarations, improvements in formatting, and bug fixes related to Media3 integration. New features include an adapter library for using Media3 effects with CameraX.

entries.title: CameraX  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the CameraX documentation, including updates to version numbers, API changes, and bug fixes. Notable changes include the migration to a high-performance, unified camera stack using CameraPipe and the integration of Media3 Muxer for improved video processing.

entries.title: Car App  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the Car App library, including new features like images in rows, enhancements to alphabetical indexing in SectionedItemTemplate, and the addition of CarIcon MediaPlayback. It also includes bug fixes, API changes, and version updates across alpha releases.

entries.title: Car  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Car has been updated. The changes include updates to the title, some formatting adjustments, and the addition of deprecation warnings for the `androidx.car:car` and `androidx.car:car-cluster` libraries. These libraries have been deprecated and developers are instructed to use `androidx.car.app` instead. This is important for developers to be aware of when working with the Android Car library.

entries.title: Cardview  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with links to the Android Developers website and related resources, improving navigation and providing more context for developers. Additionally, it has updated links to the user guide and code samples, and formatted the dependency declaration snippets for Groovy and Kotlin.

entries.title: Compose Animation
entries.summary: This document details the updates to the Compose Animation library. It reflects the release of new versions: 1.10.6 and 1.11.0-beta02, including bug fixes for SeekableTransitionState, InfiniteRepeatableSpec, custom AnimationSpec and AnimatedContent.

entries.title: Compose Compiler  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the Compose Compiler, including a move to the Kotlin repository, version updates, and bug fixes related to stability, compilation, and code generation. It also provides guidance on dependencies and feedback mechanisms.

entries.title: Compose Foundation  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes changes to the Compose Foundation library. It includes updates to API references, dependency declarations, and version updates, including bug fixes, new features, and API changes in version 1.11.0-beta02 and 1.11.0-beta01.

entries.title: Compose Material
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the latest releases of Compose Material. Specifically, it includes information on version 1.10.6 and 1.11.0-beta02, including release dates, and links to the commits. Additionally, the latest stable and beta versions were updated in the header and dependency examples to reflect the most recent releases.

entries.title: Compose Material 3 Adaptive
entries.summary: The documentation for Compose Material 3 Adaptive has been updated to reflect the latest changes in the library. Key updates include the addition of pane expansion support for ListDetailPaneScaffold and SupportingPaneScaffold, and the introduction of predictive back support for navigable scaffold implementations. Furthermore, the documentation now points to the composable functions with the correct links.

entries.title: Compose Material 3  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document underwent several updates, including changes to the title, URLs, and formatting. The most significant changes include updating the latest and alpha release versions, and updating links to the Material 3 adaptive navigation suite, plus API changes and bug fixes for the navigation suite.

entries.title: Compose Runtime  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document was updated with new stable and beta releases. It includes updated links to the latest release notes and version updates, and fixes for WASM, `LinkTable`, and `SnapshotFlowManager`.

entries.title: Compose UI  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document was updated to reflect the latest stable and beta releases of Compose UI, including API changes and bug fixes. Specifically, it includes details for 1.10.6, 1.11.0-beta01, and 1.11.0-beta02, providing links to relevant commits and issue tracker entries.

entries.title: Compose  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation updates include changes to links, updated versions, and improvements to formatting and code examples. These changes provide more accurate information about the Compose libraries, including updated version numbers and corrected links to relevant resources. Additionally, minor formatting improvements and code block adjustments enhance readability and clarity.

entries.title: Constraintlayout  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes made to the ConstraintLayout library.  Key changes include bug fixes, API updates, and new features, such as improved animation capabilities, enhanced debugging tools, and better integration with Compose and SharedTransitionLayout.

entries.title: Coordinatorlayout  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the CoordinatorLayout library. Version 1.3.0 introduces new features such as keyboard event support and demos, along with bug fixes for keyboard interactions within NestedScrollView and RecyclerView. Also, it includes the use of JSpecify nullness annotations.

entries.title: Core  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation updates include changes to the navigation links, the version of the core library, and dependency declarations. The update also includes the addition of the new `BasicPictureInPicture` and `VideoPlaybackPictureInPicture` classes, and various bug fixes and API changes related to Picture-in-Picture (PiP) functionality and improvements to the Core Backported Fixes library.

entries.title: credentials  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the credentials library has been updated to reflect the latest changes, including dependency updates, new features, API changes, and bug fixes in recent releases.  Key changes include deprecation of `credentials-e2ee`, support for Digital Credentials, and enhancements to the Credential Manager dialogs and conditional passkey creation. Various API additions and removals are also listed.

entries.title: Cursoradapter  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has undergone significant formatting and link updates.  Specifically, the title has been updated, links are now relative instead of absolute, and the dependency declaration examples were updated for improved formatting. This enhances readability and maintainability of the documentation.

entries.title: Customview  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the customview library, including updates to dependencies, API changes, and bug fixes. Notable changes include the addition of an overload for `ViewDragHelper#smoothSlideViewTo` and `@NonNull` annotations, along with improvements to the documentation and the addition of the PoolingContainer library.

entries.title: Databinding  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document, which details the Android Databinding library's releases, has undergone updates including changes to the title and URL, removal of the source from md.txt to html-scrape, improvements to link formatting, and the addition of code samples to showcase the integration of databinding with the Android Gradle plugin. These enhancements provide clearer access to related resources and examples.

entries.title: DataStore  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces changes to the DataStore documentation. Key changes include adding a new artifact for encryption support using Tink and the introduction of a new DataStore Builder API. These changes provide developers with enhanced data security and improved structured concurrency.

entries.title: DragAndDrop  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the DragAndDrop library was updated. The changes include improvements in formatting, URL updates, and clarifications.  Specifically, there are more concise descriptions, and improvements to links.

entries.title: Emoji  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes changes to the Emoji library. Key updates include the release of version 1.2.0, with the addition of GlyphChecker, and various bug fixes and API improvements.  Additionally, the document now contains links to the new version 1.2.0-rc01 and updated the stable release to version 1.2.0.

entries.title: Enterprise  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the Enterprise Jetpack library, including information on dependencies, feedback, and version history.  The main changes involve updating links, formatting, and refining descriptions of the library's features and API changes.

entries.title: Exifinterface  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details changes to the Exifinterface library, including bug fixes for JPEG, WebP, PNG, and HEIF/AVIF image handling, as well as improvements to XMP data management and nullability annotations.  It also addresses issues related to  invalid IFD offsets, and  WebP image corruption.

entries.title: Fragment
entries.summary: The documentation for Fragments has been updated to reflect the new `AndroidFragment` composable, which allows adding fragments to the Compose hierarchy. Also, the documentation has been updated to use the new `NOTE` markdown syntax.

entries.title: Android Games  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation updates include changes to links, formatting, and the addition and modification of content related to the Android Game SDK and its associated libraries.  These changes cover updates to dependency declarations, Gradle properties, and CMake integration, as well as version updates and bug fixes for the various game libraries (games-memory-advice, games-text-input, and games-activity).  These updates ensure that developers have the correct information to integrate and utilize the Android Game SDK libraries effectively.

entries.title: Glance  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the Glance Jetpack library, including dependency updates, bug fixes, and API additions.  Key updates include the release of version 1.2.0-rc01 and 1.2.0-beta01, along with the deprecation of `currentCompositeKeyHash` and addition of preview and alpha control APIs for images.

entries.title: Health  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation updates include changes to the API reference links, and the addition of links to the Android Developers website and other related resources. There are also several changes to the API, including the addition of new unit types, renaming of classes, and bug fixes related to Proguard and SDK requirements.

entries.title: Heifwriter  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the Heifwriter library. The primary change is the introduction of the EncoderPreference class in version 1.2.0-alpha01, allowing developers to specify encoder preferences like hardware/software encoding and Constant Quantization (CQ), and fixes related to JSpecify nullness annotations and buffer queue release in version 1.1.0.

entries.title: Hilt  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the Hilt documentation, including the addition of a new alpha release (1.4.0-alpha01) and updates to existing releases (1.3.0 and 1.2.0), along with minor content adjustments and link updates.

entries.title: Interpolator  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with minor formatting changes and link updates. These changes include updates to the title, URLs, and formatting of code blocks and tables. There are no functional changes to the library itself.

entries.title: JavascriptEngine  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document was updated to reflect the latest changes in the JavascriptEngine library. These changes include updating the dependency versions and introducing message ports API for enhanced communication with JavaScript isolates.

entries.title: Jetifier  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces updates to the Jetifier tool, including new features and bug fixes. The changes mainly involve the addition of a timestamp policy, upgrade to ASM 8, support for `@link` references in XML, improvements to handle mixed androidx and android.support references, and various bug fixes related to ProGuard parsing, signature errors, and mapping issues for different libraries.

entries.title: Legacy  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with a new title, navigation links, and formatting changes.  The core content regarding the deprecation of the Legacy API and the feedback section remain largely unchanged.  These changes improve the document's overall presentation and user navigation.

entries.title: Lifecycle  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the Lifecycle library. The main changes include updates to the scoped ViewModels for Compose, new features, and API changes. Version 2.11.0-alpha03 includes API changes related to `ViewModelStoreProvider`, `ViewModelStoreNavEntryDecorator`, and new extension functions for retrieving ViewModels and initializing with default `SavedState` values, as well as bug fixes. Version 2.11.0-alpha02 introduced scoped ViewModel creation for Compose, new features such as KMP target support, factory functions and extension functions to reduce boilerplate.

entries.title: lint  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the lint checks for Gradle API usage. Several updates include new features and bug fixes across different alpha versions, with the latest alpha release introducing checks to prevent Gradle configuration issues and catch internal Kotlin Gradle Plugin API usage.

entries.title: Localbroadcastmanager  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for LocalBroadcastManager has been updated to reflect its deprecation and recommend alternatives. It emphasizes that the library is deprecated and suggests using LiveData or reactive streams instead of LocalBroadcastManager. The update also includes changes to formatting and link updates.

entries.title: Media  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the Media library. The changes include improvements in performance and bug fixes.

entries.title: Media3  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Media3 library version 1.10.0, including the updates from 1.10.0-rc03. The release includes API reference updates, dependency declarations, and detailed changes across multiple modules such as Common, ExoPlayer, CompositionPlayer, Transformer, Track selection, Extractors, Inspector, Audio, Text, Metadata, Effect, IMA extension, Session, UI, HLS extension, DASH extension, RTSP extension, Decoder extensions, and Cast extension, as well as removal of deprecated symbols. These changes provide improvements in stability, new features (like Dolby Vision Profile 10 support), bug fixes, and performance enhancements across various aspects of media playback and processing.

entries.title: Mediarouter  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation updates include changes to the API, bug fixes, and improvements in the MediaRouter library. These changes provide support for device suggestions, permission-based route visibility, and new functionality for route connection and stream expansion. They also address issues like playback routing and UI fixes.

entries.title: Metrics  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for androidx.metrics has been updated with various changes, including improved formatting, link updates, and bug fixes in recent releases.  Significant API changes include performance improvements with more accurate timing information, and changes to the JankStats API regarding the handling of frame data and thread management for improved performance and reduced overhead.

entries.title: Multidex  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Multidex has been updated to include a new title, improve formatting, and clarify how to provide feedback. The changes include updates to the links, the addition of a 'Stay organized with collections' section, and adjustments to the feedback section. These changes aim to enhance the user experience and provide clearer instructions for reporting issues and accessing related documentation.

entries.title: photopicker  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update introduces the initial alpha release of the androidx implementations for the Embedded PhotoPicker, including composable and view-based entry points, state management options, and a testing provider. This enables easier integration of the Embedded Photopicker service in both View-based and Compose-based Android applications.

entries.title: privacysandbox ads  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to the privacysandbox ads library.  The changes include new features for Custom Audience Priority, Seller Configuration, and Delayed Custom Audience Updates. There are also API changes and updates related to deprecated APIs and missing constructor overloads.

entries.title: privacysandbox plugins  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes changes to the Android Privacy Sandbox plugins, including updates to library versions (tools, SDK runtime, and coroutines), and a new Androidx Gradle Plugin to assist with Android libraries used by privacy sandbox SDK modules. These changes provide improvements and support for developers working with Privacy Sandbox SDKs.

entries.title: privacysandbox  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the deprecation of the privacysandbox-tools library and includes various updates and fixes across different alpha versions. The document also includes updates to the links and formatting.

entries.title: ProfileInstaller  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes made to the ProfileInstaller library. Key changes include fixes for multi-process Baseline Profile recording and updates to support API 35, along with other bug fixes.

entries.title: Recyclerview  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for RecyclerView has been updated to reflect the latest changes, including new features and API changes in the 1.4.0 release. Key changes include adaptive refresh rate support, a new API for checking if a layout is reversed, deprecation of some APIs, and improvements to tracing and compilation.

entries.title: AndroidX Core library release notes  |  Core areas  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This release includes several updates to the AndroidX Core library. Notably, it introduces new `LocalePreferences` APIs for easier access to locale data and user preferences. Additionally, compatibility methods have been added to align with SDK 34 for classes such as `TextView` and `AccessibilityNodeInfo`.

entries.title: Remotecallback  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the Remotecallback library. The changes include updates to the title, introduction of navigation links, and formatting updates. It also includes API changes and a deprecation notice for the Slices framework.

entries.title: Room  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has undergone significant updates. It includes updates to the Room library's documentation, including improved formatting, corrected links, and the addition of details about new features and bug fixes. The changes aim to clarify and enhance the usability of the documentation.

entries.title: Room 3.0  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the Room 3.0 documentation. The document has been updated to reflect the latest release (3.0.0-alpha02), which includes features like FTS5 support, new targets in Room Paging, API changes (multi-platform clearAllTables, destructive migration defaults, and experimental APIs), and bug fixes. The document also includes the changes introduced in 3.0.0-alpha01 and explains the core concepts and breaking changes from Room 2.x, emphasizing Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP) support, removal of SupportSQLite APIs, and the requirement for Kotlin Symbol Processing (KSP).

entries.title: Savedstate  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This diff updates the Savedstate documentation with the latest releases, including version 1.5.0-alpha02. It also includes the update of the links to the user guide and API reference.

entries.title: ShareTarget  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for ShareTarget has been updated to reflect the latest stable release (1.2.0) with fixes for Android 12 compatibility regarding the `android:exported` attribute. Additionally, the documentation now includes links for Jetpack and developer documentation and a change in formatting for code examples and links.

entries.title: Slidingpanelayout  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the SlidingPaneLayout library. Key updates include fold-aware behavior for foldable devices, improved UI styling, default closing of the pane, and the ability to register multiple PanelSlideListeners. Additionally, developers can now control user swipe behavior with a lock mode.

entries.title: Swiperefreshlayout  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in the swiperefreshlayout.md documentation. The main changes include updates to the version numbers, dependency declarations, and bug fixes.

entries.title: Swiperefreshlayout  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation was updated with the release of version 1.2.0, including a bug fix related to the refresh icon and the correction of an issue with the requestDisallowInterceptTouchEvent(boolean) method, ensuring it behaves as expected within a ViewGroup.

entries.title: Swiperefreshlayout  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Version 1.2.0-beta01 was released, which included specifying the nullability of SwipeRefreshLayout method parameters and return types, and the use of JSpecify nullness annotations.

entries.title: Swiperefreshlayout  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for version 1.1.0-alpha03 of SwipeRefreshLayout was updated, including a new style attribute to set the background color of the progress indicator and a fix for nested scrolling issues.

entries.title: text-vertical  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes made to the text-vertical library in the Jetpack androidx project. The changes include adding horizontal layout support for special spans in Japanese text, API changes for `RubySpan` and `EmphasisSpan`, a new method to check vertical text support, graceful fallback for older APIs, and a downgrade of `minSDK`.

entries.title: Tracing  |  Jetpack  |  Android Developers

entries.title: Godot Engine Vulkan optimization for Android  |  Developer stories  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The Godot Vulkan renderer was refactored to improve performance across all platforms. Improvements include dynamic UBO offset support, linear descriptor set pools to reduce overhead, and immutable sampler support to optimize sampler management. These changes increase efficiency and pave the way for future optimizations.

entries.title: Godot Engine Vulkan optimization for Android  |  Developer stories  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Optimizations were implemented to boost rendering performance specifically on mobile hardware. These involved replacing large push constants with uniform buffers, employing lazy buffer allocation for memory savings, adding persistent buffer support to eliminate unnecessary data transfers, changing ASTC decode mode, and implementing screen pre-rotation.

entries.title: Kuro Games reduces 9.68% power consumption through Android Studio Power Profiler and ODPM for Wuthering Waves  |  Developer stories  |  Android Developers

They achieved a 9.68% reduction in power consumption by developing custom tools and implementing several optimizations such as changing CPU core scheduling, pre-compiling PSO, and implementing PVS and offline shadow occlusion culling.

entries.summary: This update modifies the document's metadata, including the title, and adds navigation links to Android developer resources. This improves navigation and provides easier access to relevant documentation for developers.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update to the Android Studio release notes for version 1.0.1 includes minor formatting adjustments and clarifies the process for switching to the Stable update channel, ensuring users receive production-ready updates. The core functionality updates remain the same, focusing on resolving issues related to the AVD Manager, emulator logs on Windows, and AVD creation on different drives.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document's metadata has been updated to reflect the new website's structure and source. The document's title and URL were modified to point to the correct Android Developers website. This change matters because it ensures the document correctly links to the intended Android Studio release notes on the official Android Developers website.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Android Studio version 1.1.0, including support for Android Wear watch templates, the use of `res/mipmap` folders for launcher icons, Material Design updates for launcher icons, enhanced Lint checks, and support for BCP language tag 47. These updates improve the development experience by providing better tools and resources for building Android applications.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The release notes for Android Studio v1.2.1 have been updated. The changes include a modification to the title and URL, along with the addition of navigation links and a note on how to stay organized using collections.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces Android Studio v1.2.2, released in June 2015. The update addresses build issues that were preventing builds from finishing.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update to the Android Studio release notes for version 1.3.1 primarily addresses formatting and includes minor content adjustments. Specifically, it corrects the title, updates the document source, and removes unnecessary HTML tags. The core content, detailing fixes and enhancements, remains largely unchanged.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update to the Android Studio release notes includes several bug fixes and enhancements. Key changes include adding support for Android 6.0, fixing an exception during update checks, resolving layout editor crashes, addressing invalid resource type warnings, and correcting a lint check issue related to resource privacy. These changes improve stability and functionality for Android developers.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android Studio 1.4.0 introduced several new features. It included the Vector Asset Studio for importing vector graphics, new GPU and Network functions in the Android Monitor, an early preview of the Theme Editor, and updated Android templates for the Design Support Library. These enhancements improve developer workflows by providing tools for managing assets, monitoring performance, and designing user interfaces based on Material Design.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the fixes and enhancements made in Android Studio v1.4.1. The changes address issues related to Gradle syncing, native debugging, Subversion compatibility, Device Chooser functionality, translation error reporting, Theme Editor interactions, and manifest attribute handling. These fixes improve the stability and usability of the IDE.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android Studio 1.5.0 introduces enhancements to the Memory Monitor for easier memory leak detection, new deep linking and app linking support within the Code Editor, improved Vector Asset Studio compatibility, and new lint checks for Android TV, Android Auto, and security vulnerabilities. These updates aim to improve development efficiency, app quality, and security.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the fixes and enhancements made in Android Studio v1.5.1, released in December 2015. Key changes include fixes for layout rendering failures, the ability to configure manifest attributes, improvements to the Darcula theme in Vector Asset Studio, the addition of a Help button in Vector Asset Studio, support for the % operator in data binding, and fixes for debugging and null pointer exceptions.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android Studio 2.0 (April 2016) introduces Instant Run for faster build and deployment cycles, significant Lint improvements for code quality, and enhancements to the Android Emulator and Virtual Device Manager, along with integration with Cloud Test Lab and Google App Indexing.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Android Studio 2.2 introduces several new features including a new Layout Editor with ConstraintLayout support, an APK Analyzer, the Espresso Test Recorder, C/C++ build integration, and the Samples Browser. It also includes updates to the Android Emulator and Run/Debug Configuration options, and it bundles OpenJDK 8. Additionally, there are minor updates with bug fixes in 2.2.1, 2.2.2 and 2.2.3.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Android Studio 3.1, including updates to the bundled Kotlin version, bug fixes, and improvements to SQL editing with Room, data binding, and the default DEX compiler (D8).  The document also details new features and improvements for coding, the IDE, compiler and Gradle.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Android Studio 3.2 release notes. The changes include updates to Kotlin, build tools, the Navigation library, and bug fixes related to data binding and other components. It also details new features like the What's New Assistant, Android Jetpack integration, Android App Bundle support, sample data in Layout Editor, Slices, and improvements to the Android Profilers.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the release notes for Android Studio 3.3, including updates to the IDE, Android Gradle plugin, Navigation Editor, Profiler, and support for Clang-Tidy. It also includes improvements in the areas of UI, memory, and networking.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the release notes for Android Studio 3.6, including updates to design tools, the Android Gradle plugin, native tooling, and debugging capabilities. These updates provide developers with improved functionality for UI design, build speed optimization, and debugging native code and memory leaks.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Design editors now offer a Split view, allowing simultaneous Design and Code views, and the zoom/pan controls are in a floating panel.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The Color Picker now populates color resource values within the IDE to help you quickly update color resource values in your app when you're using the color picker in your XML or the design tools.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The Resource Manager now supports more resource types, displays results from all project modules when searching, has filtering options, and allows renaming resources during import.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: View binding is introduced to simplify interacting with views in code and eliminates the risk of null pointer exceptions. You can enable it by adding a line of code into your build.gradle file.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The options to apply changes (Apply Code Changes and Apply Changes and Restart Activity) are explained, providing a simpler way to see your code changes in action.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: A refactor option has been added for Instant Apps support, letting developers easily enable Instant Apps from the IDE.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The APK Analyzer now supports deobfuscation of class and method bytecode, enhancing the ability to inspect DEX files.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: Enhanced support for native development, including Kotlin support for JNI, improved JNI development workflow, and APK reloading for native libraries.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: It is now possible to attach Kotlin-only external APK sources when you profile and debug pre-built APKs.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The Memory Profiler now includes a filter to identify potential memory leaks for Activity and Fragment instances.

entries.title: Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes in Android Studio 4.1 release notes, including updates to the Database Inspector, Emulator, TensorFlow Lite models, Native Memory Profiler, System Trace UI, and standalone profilers.  It explains the features, improvements, and known issues.

entries.title: Android Studio Arctic Fox | 2020.3.1 (July 2021)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the release notes for Android Studio Arctic Fox (2020.3.1) released in July 2021. It highlights changes in version numbering, the Android Gradle plugin (AGP), and new features such as the Background Task Inspector and improvements to the Database Inspector, including data export functionality. The changes aim to improve build speed, debugging capabilities, and data management within Android Studio.

entries.title: Android Studio Chipmunk | 2021.2.1 (May 2022)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the changes made to the Android Studio Chipmunk release notes. It includes updates to the title and links, and fixes to the formatting of the tables. It also updates the links to point to the correct documentation.

entries.title: Android Studio Dolphin | 2021.3.1 (Sep 2022)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the new features and improvements in Android Studio Dolphin. Key updates include enhancements to Compose tooling (animation coordination, multipreview annotations, and recomposition counts), updated Wear OS emulator features (pairing assistant, toolbar, and run configurations), and a redesigned Logcat with improved formatting, multi-window support, and a new key-value search. Gradle Managed Devices for automated testing are also introduced.

entries.title: Android Studio Flamingo | 2022.2.1 (April 2023)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the new features and improvements in Android Studio Flamingo 2022.2.1, including updates to themed app icons, dynamic color previews, Live Edit, Compose Tracing, project templates, device mirroring, and the Network Inspector.

entries.title: Android Studio Giraffe | 2022.3.1 (July 2023)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the new features and improvements in Android Studio Giraffe. Key updates include Live Edit enhancements, a new UI preview option, expanded Compose Animation Preview API support, integration of the Grammatical Inflection API, improvements to the Device Explorer, a new Android SDK Upgrade Assistant, enhanced diagnostic tools, a 'Make Selected Modules' toolbar button, and download information during Gradle sync.

entries.title: Android Studio Hedgehog | 2023.1.1 (Nov 2023)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the new features and improvements in Android Studio Hedgehog 2023.1.1, including updates to App Quality Insights with Android Vitals data, a new Power Profiler, enhancements to the App Links Assistant, Live Edit, Compose tooling improvements (Multipreview templates, Preview Gallery Mode, Compose state information in debugger), Device mirroring, and the Embedded Layout Inspector. These features aim to enhance debugging, UI development, and overall developer productivity.

entries.title: Android Studio Iguana | 2023.2.1 (Feb 2024)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the new features and improvements in Android Studio Iguana (2023.2.1) and its patch releases. Key changes include integration with Version Control System in App Quality Insights, viewing Crashlytics crash variants in App Quality Insights, Compose UI Check improvements, Progressive Rendering for Compose Preview, a Baseline Profiles module wizard, and the Espresso Device API for testing configuration changes.

entries.title: Android Studio Jellyfish | 2023.3.1 (Apr 2024)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This update to Android Studio Jellyfish (2023.3.1) includes critical security fixes for the GitHub plugin, addressing a vulnerability that could expose access tokens.  It also incorporates several improvements and new features, including enhancements to App Quality Insights, an embedded Layout Inspector, App Links Assistant updates, automatic Baseline Profile installation, new colorblind checks in Compose UI Check, and audio redirection for device mirroring.

entries.title: Android Studio Koala | 2024.1.1 (June 2024)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document was updated to reflect Android Studio Koala 2024.1.1, including patch release information, and provides links to bug fixes. It also highlights the Gemini API template for building generative AI apps.

entries.title: Android Studio Meerkat Feature Drop | 2024.3.2 (May 2025)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the new features in Android Studio Meerkat Feature Drop. Key changes include enhanced themed icon support with preview capabilities, modifications to Android Studio configuration directory paths for improved release management, the introduction of a Prompt Library for Gemini, and the deprecation of the Motion Editor in favor of Jetpack Compose for animations.

entries.title: Android Studio Narwhal 3 Feature Drop | 2025.1.3 (September 2025)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the new features introduced in Android Studio Narwhal 3 Feature Drop. Key changes include enhancements to Compose previews with resizable windows and a resize toolbar, integration of Play Policy Insights within the IDE, tools for testing app backup and restore functionalities, and options for Proguard inspections, and a new manual project sync mode.

entries.title: Android Studio Narwhal | 2025.1.1 (June 2025)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the new features introduced in Android Studio Narwhal (June 2025), focusing on improvements to Gemini in Android Studio for businesses, the introduction of Studio Labs for experimental AI features, and enhancements to the Embedded Layout Inspector component tree. The changes include updates to the UI, improved navigation, and more explicit visual cues to help developers understand and refine their UI.

entries.title: Android Studio Otter 2 Feature Drop (December 2025)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the new features in Android Studio Otter 2 Feature Drop, including an updated title and a new section for the Android Knowledge Base. This knowledge base provides access to up-to-date documentation from various sources, enhancing the IDE's ability to provide accurate and relevant information. Additionally, there is a new feature for communications opt-in.

entries.title: Android Studio Otter 3 Feature Drop (January 2026)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the new features available in Android Studio Otter 3 Feature Drop (January 2026). Key improvements include AI-assisted App Links implementation, enhanced UI development with Gemini, improvements to Gemini's chat features, automatic Logcat retracing, and support for remote model providers.

entries.title: Android Studio Otter | 2025.2.1 (October 2025)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the new features in Android Studio Otter. The primary change is the introduction of a Backup and Sync feature that allows users to back up and synchronize Android Studio settings, such as keymaps and editor settings, to cloud storage using either a Google or JetBrains account. This feature enhances user experience by enabling consistent IDE configurations across multiple installations.

entries.title: Android Studio Panda 1 (February 2026)  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the new features in Android Studio Panda 1. Key changes include improvements to JDK management using Gradle Daemon JVM Criteria, simplifying project setup, and ensuring consistent builds. The document also provides a new image illustrating the Gradle build execution flow and updates the note regarding project Daemon JVM criteria configuration.

entries.summary: This document describes the emulator console commands. The changes include updates to the title, updated links, formatting changes, and some rephrasing for clarity.  Additionally, there are minor changes in the formatting of the command descriptions.

entries.summary: The 'Cellular' section of the emulator extended controls documentation was updated to include '5G' as a selectable network type, providing users with the ability to simulate and test their applications in a 5G network environment.

entries.title: Launch the emulator in a separate window  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes how to launch the Android Emulator in a separate window. It explains the default behavior of the emulator running within Android Studio and how to configure it to run in a separate window to access certain features. The main change involves updating the title and adding a navigation bar.

entries.summary: The document's title and URL have been updated.  Additionally, the document now includes a series of links to relevant Android developer resources and slightly improved formatting of the instructions, including image paths. These changes enhance the discoverability and clarity of the content.

entries.title: Advanced emulator networking features  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides advanced emulator networking features, detailing command-line options for the network simulator. It explains how to use flags like `--logtostderr`, `--verbose`, `--hci-port`, `--pcap`, `--wifi`, and `--rssi` to configure the network behavior, capture packets, and set Wi-Fi access points and received signal strength (RSSI) for Bluetooth radios. These features are useful for debugging and testing network-related issues within the Android emulator.

entries.title: Configure emulator DNS settings  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The document's title and URL were updated. Formatting was adjusted. A code block was added to show the command-line usage. These changes provide clarity and improve the document's usability.

entries.title: Set up Proxy with the Android Emulator  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document, originally titled "emulator-networking-proxy.md", has been updated with a new title, improved formatting, and updated links. The primary content focuses on configuring proxies within the Android Emulator for application debugging and bypassing firewalls. Key changes include updated image paths, revised configuration instructions, and improved clarity on the use cases and limitations of each proxy type. Additionally, the document now includes links to related Android developer resources.

entries.title: Android Emulator networking  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with a new title, added navigation links, and minor formatting changes to the table. These changes help in providing clearer navigation and better context for the content.

entries.title: Record the screen  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for recording the Android Emulator screen was updated. The title and URL were updated. Additional links to other Android developer resources were added. entries.title: Snapshots  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces minor updates to the emulator snapshots documentation. The changes include updating image paths, minor rewording, and improved formatting for enhanced clarity. Additionally, there are updates to the snapshot requirements and troubleshooting section.

entries.title: Camera support  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the new title and source of the document. The document now also includes a list of links to other Android developer resources. The changes also include minor formatting adjustments in the section about virtual scene camera and ARCore.

entries.title: Run apps on the Android Emulator  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the Android Emulator, explaining its purpose in simulating Android devices for app testing. It outlines the benefits of using the emulator, including flexibility, high fidelity, and speed, and guides users through the basic steps of getting started with it.

entries.title: Create and manage virtual devices  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces updates to the Android Virtual Device (AVD) management guide. The changes include updates to the title, improved navigation, and minor updates to the images and instructions on how to manage AVDs.

entries.title: Install OEM USB drivers  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides instructions on installing OEM USB drivers for Android devices on Windows. It includes updated installation steps for Windows 10, 8.1, and 7, along with a table of OEM driver download links. It also includes general information on where to find the drivers for other operating systems.

entries.title: Test on multiple screen sizes with the resizable emulator  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the resizable emulator feature in Android Studio. The key change is the removal of the experimental status for the resizable emulator, indicating its increased stability and readiness for general use. Additionally, there are changes to the title and URL of the document, as well as minor formatting and image path updates. This change matters because it signifies a more mature and reliable tool for developers to test their apps on various screen sizes, improving the development and testing workflow.

entries.title: Create and edit run/debug configurations  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to run/debug configurations in Android Studio. The title and URL are updated, and the document is now scraped from HTML. It incorporates new links to relevant Android developer resources, reorganizes content with a more concise format and improved section headers, and includes images directly within the text. Additionally, it clarifies how to create, edit, and save configurations, along with explanations of the various configuration templates and how to define configurations for different project items.

entries.title: Service integrations in Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Studio service integrations has been updated to include a new title and a set of links to other Android developer resources. It also includes a more detailed description of the permissions required for various cloud services, such as Gemini Code Assist, Firebase Device Streaming, and Crashlytics.

entries.title: Deprecated service integrations  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document details the deprecation of several Android Studio service integrations.  Specifically, it highlights the deprecation of Meerkat (Cloud Source Repositories project import and App Engine support) and Narwhal Feature Drop (Run with Firebase Test Lab) features, indicating their removal in future Android Studio versions.  These changes impact developers who rely on these specific integrations for their development workflow.

entries.title: Terms and conditions  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation's title and source were updated. Additionally, the updated documentation now includes links to Android Developers and Android Studio along with a short description.

entries.title: Test from the command line  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document, which explains how to run tests from the command line, has been updated. The changes include updates to links and formatting to match current documentation standards, as well as the inclusion of code blocks within the document.

entries.title: Test against screen configuration changes with the Espresso Device API  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the Espresso Device API, which enables UI testing against device configuration changes such as rotation and screen unfolding. It provides setup instructions, including required dependencies and configurations in the build files, along with example code snippets demonstrating how to write tests for different device states and how to specify device requirements for tests.

entries.title: MonkeyDevice  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for MonkeyDevice was updated. Changes include modifications to the title, introduction, and formatting of method and constant tables, improving readability and consistency with Android Developers documentation standards.

entries.title: MonkeyImage  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for MonkeyImage has been updated with minor formatting changes, including improved table formatting and more descriptive argument explanations. The main content and functionality of the methods remain unchanged.

entries.title: Other testing tools  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation's title and URL have been updated to reflect the new format. It also includes links to Android Developers and Android Studio. Formatting has been improved for better readability.

entries.title: Create UI tests with Espresso Test Recorder  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document describes the Espresso Test Recorder tool, which allows users to create UI tests for Android applications without writing code. It explains how to record UI interactions, add assertions to verify UI elements, and save the recording to generate an Espresso test. The update includes formatting changes, updated links and a new screenshot.

entries.title: UI/Application Exerciser Monkey  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for the UI/Application Exerciser Monkey has been updated. These changes don't significantly alter the technical content but enhance the presentation and organization of the document.

entries.summary: The documentation for the `android` command-line tool has been updated. Formatting and minor wording changes were also made for clarity and consistency.

entries.title: Add app resources  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces updates to the "Add app resources" documentation. The changes include improvements in formatting, URL updates, and enhancements to dialog descriptions and resource merging explanations. These modifications improve clarity and accuracy in describing how to add and manage resources in Android Studio.

entries.title: Improve code inspection with annotations  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides information about annotations in Android development, which help improve code inspection and prevent errors. entries.title: Create WebP images  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation for creating WebP images in Android Studio has been updated. entries.title: Create app icons  |  Android Studio  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to adaptive and legacy launcher icons, action bar, tab, and notification icons. It also includes improvements in how Image Asset Studio is used within Android Studio.

These changes provide more direct context and navigation within the Android developer ecosystem.

The document also includes instructions on how to enable the Orchestrator using Gradle, Android Studio, and command line.

entries.title: Performance tests  |  Test your app on Android  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces performance testing on Android, covering both field testing and local testing methodologies. It highlights tools like Google Play Vitals, Firebase Performance Monitoring, AndroidX Tracing, and JankStats for gathering performance metrics, along with macrobenchmark and microbenchmark libraries for local analysis. The document also offers a few use cases for performance test results, such as performance improvement, regression avoidance, and production monitoring.

entries.title: Build local unit tests  |  Test your app on Android  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on building local unit tests in Android, emphasizing their role in testing code on a local JVM. The changes include updates to the title and URL, addition of navigation links, code formatting, and minor text refinements.

entries.title: Robolectric strategies  |  Test your app on Android  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces Robolectric, an open-source framework for testing Android applications. The changes include updates to the title and URL, addition of navigation links, code formatting updates, and minor adjustments to the key point about Robolectric usage. These changes clarify Robolectric's role in UI testing and provide guidance on its appropriate use, particularly when considering testable architectures.

entries.title: Use the UI Automator legacy API  |  Test your app on Android  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the UI Automator legacy API, emphasizing its role in cross-app UI testing on Android, while also strongly recommending the use of the newer UI Automator API for new development. The document includes key features, API usage, setup instructions, and accessibility considerations for effective UI testing. Changes include minor text updates, improved code formatting, and updated links.

entries.title: Write automated tests with UI Automator  |  Test your app on Android  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces the modern UI Automator testing framework with a Kotlin-friendly DSL. entries.title: Use Jetpack Compose on Wear OS

Additionally, the example code now includes the `minimumVerticalContentPadding` modifier, and the instructions for adding snap-and-fling behavior have been updated to use `TransformingLazyColumnDefaults.snapFlingBehavior(columnState)` instead of the previous method with `ScalingLazyColumn`.

entries.title: Wear OS 4 overview  |  Android Developers

entries.title: Prepare your app for behavior changes in Wear OS 4  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines behavior changes in Wear OS 4, based on Android 13, and their potential impact on app development. It highlights changes related to permissions (notification, background body sensors, and approximate location), app components and navigation (intent filters, root launcher activity, app links), and power/data management (restricted app standby bucket, app hibernation, and backup/restore). Developers need to understand and address these changes to ensure app compatibility and functionality on Wear OS 4 devices. Notably, the document points out the need to handle notification runtime permissions, adjust intent filters, and adapt to system changes regarding app standby and hibernation.

entries.title: Explore features in Wear OS 4  |  Android Developers

entries.title: Update your app's target SDK version for Wear OS 5  |  Wear OS 5  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the latest changes in the Wear OS 5 SDK, specifically mentioning the need to target Android 14 (API level 34) for optimal compatibility. The code examples for updating the build file have been updated and some of the links have been updated to the correct relative paths.

entries.title: Test how your app handles behavior changes  |  Wear OS 6  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document outlines changes for Wear OS 6, based on Android 16 (API level 36). Key updates include improvements to always-on behavior, font consistency for tiles, more granular health permissions, and batched tile interaction events. These changes impact app behavior and require developers to adapt their apps to maintain compatibility and leverage new features.

entries.title: Explore features  |  Wear OS 6  |  Android Developers

entries.title: SDK Platform Tools release notes
entries.summary: Platform Tools revision 37.0.0 introduces improvements to `adb`. The default mDNS backend has been changed to `libadbmdns`, with `openscreen` as a deprecated failover. entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 17 or higher
entries.summary: This document outlines behavior changes introduced in Android 17, focusing on performance, security, and privacy. Key changes include a new lock-free implementation of MessageQueue, restrictions on `static final` fields, improvements to accessibility features, enforcement of local network permissions, a new setting for hiding passwords on physical devices, and security enhancements like enabling Certificate Transparency by default and extending Safer Dynamic Code Loading protection to native libraries. It also describes changes related to orientation and resizability restrictions for large screens.

entries.title: Restrictions on orientation and resizability are ignored
entries.summary: The documentation for ff-restrictions-ignored.md was updated to specify that orientation, resizability, and aspect ratio restrictions are ignored for apps targeting Android 17 (API level 37) or higher, on displays with a smallest width greater than 600dp. This clarifies the API level requirement for the described behavior.

entries.title: MessageQueue behavior change guidance
entries.summary: This document describes changes to the `android.os.MessageQueue` implementation in Android 17. The update introduces a lock-free implementation for improved performance, which might affect apps that use reflection on `MessageQueue`. The document clarifies that the new behavior is enabled by default for apps targeting API level 37 and provides instructions on how to test and temporarily disable the new implementation.

entries.title: Features and APIs
entries.summary: Android 17 introduces two significant new features: PQC APK Signing and a Dedicated Assistant Volume Stream. PQC APK Signing enhances app security against potential quantum computing attacks by introducing a hybrid APK signature scheme. The Dedicated Assistant Volume Stream provides isolated volume control for Assistant apps, improving user experience and allowing for more flexible audio management.

entries.title: Release notes

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 17 or higher
entries.summary: Android 17 introduces changes related to user privacy and security. The major change involves the introduction of the `ACCESS_LOCAL_NETWORK` runtime permission, which is required for apps targeting Android 17 or higher to access the local network, enhancing user privacy by preventing unauthorized access. Another notable change is the refined Background Activity Launch (BAL) restrictions. These changes aim to enhance user security and privacy.

It also includes code samples and considerations for implementing these transitions.

entries.title: Register for limited distribution on Android devices

entries.title: Back up large amounts of data with the Android Large Backups API Program

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 17 or higher

This change aims to improve system stability and security by preventing unexpected modification of constant values.
