The update includes a new alpha release (1.0.0-alpha06) with API changes, such as the addition of a factory method for RegisterExportRequest and the removal of unused helper methods for ImportCredentialsException.

entries.title: credentials
entries.summary: The documentation for the AndroidX Credentials library was updated to reflect the latest release information. Specifically, the 'Latest Update' date was updated, and the release of 'credentials-e2ee' version 1.0.0-alpha03 was added, including a deprecation notice for `credentials:credentials-e2ee`.

entries.title: DataStore
entries.summary: This document was updated to reflect the latest DataStore releases. It includes details about the release of DataStore 1.2.1, 1.3.0-alpha07 and related dependency updates, including new features such as encryption support via Tink and a new `DataStore.Builder` API.

entries.title: Android Games
entries.summary: This update reflects the release of new versions for `games-activity` and `games-text-input`, specifically versions 4.4.0 and 4.3.0, respectively. It also includes an update to the last updated date and the addition of links to the commit logs for the latest releases. These changes provide the latest stable versions and important context regarding the library's development.

entries.title: Glance Wear
entries.summary: This document outlines the changes in the Glance Wear library.  The update includes the release of version 1.0.0-alpha05, which introduces a new API for Wear Widget background colors and removes the `fetchActiveWidgetsForProvider` method.

entries.title: graphics
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the release of graphics-path 1.1.0-rc01 and also includes the latest update date. Additionally, there are updates to the dependency declarations using Groovy and Kotlin.

entries.title: ink
entries.summary: This update introduces version 1.1.0-alpha01 of the Ink library, including new features, API changes, and bug fixes. It also includes the release of version 1.0.0, with bug fixes, and details releases from 1.0.0-rc01 to 1.0.0-alpha05. The changes involve improvements to API, and bug fixes.

entries.title: JavascriptEngine
entries.summary: This document introduces version 1.1.0-alpha01 of the JavascriptEngine library, released on March 11, 2026. This alpha release includes bug fixes related to Java 11 compatibility and the use of JSpecify nullness annotations.

entries.title: Lifecycle
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the AndroidX Lifecycle library, specifically focusing on version 2.11.0-alpha02. Key changes include the release of new APIs for scoped `ViewModel` instances in Compose, enabling ViewModels to be tied to specific UI scopes.  This version also introduces new features like support for all KMP targets in ViewModel Navigation3, factory functions for lifecycle owners, and lambda-based lifecycle observer extensions, along with various API additions to support scoped ViewModels and providers.

entries.title: Navigation
entries.summary: The documentation for AndroidX Navigation has been updated to include version 2.10.0-alpha01, released on March 11, 2026. This release introduces new features for predictive back gesture transitions and cross-platform support, along with a minimum SDK update. Additionally, several "Note" and "Caution" sections have been updated to use the "Note" and "Caution" blockquotes.

entries.title: navigation3
entries.summary: The documentation for Navigation 3 has been updated to reflect the release of version 1.1.0-beta01. This update includes the new release date and updated dependency declarations for both Groovy and Kotlin, shifting the dependencies from alpha to beta.

entries.title: Paging
entries.summary: This document introduces version 3.5.0-alpha01 of the Paging library, which includes new features for more flexible data handling and UI integration.  Key additions are a new `asState` flow operator and `append`/`prepend` APIs for `Pager`, providing developers with more control over data loading and state management, as well as new APIs for error handling. These changes enable features such as combining paged data with other data sources, sharing data across multiple collectors without caching, and loading data at the beginning or end of the list.

entries.title: pdf
entries.summary: This update includes the release of androidx.pdf:pdf-viewer-fragment:1.0.0-alpha15. The changes include API changes, such as adding Android SDK Extension Version 19 as a requirement and deprecating `isLinearized`. Bug fixes address issues with the fast scroller and widget interactions, and the libraries now target Java 11.

entries.title: Room 3.0
entries.summary: This document introduces Room 3.0, a new major version of the Room persistence library, focusing on Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP) and introducing changes such as a new package, Coroutine APIs, and support for JavaScript and WasmJS.

entries.title: Savedstate
entries.summary: This update introduces SavedState library version 1.5.0-alpha01, including new features like generic serialization for SavedState objects and extension functions for Kotlin Serializable objects, plus factory functions to reduce boilerplate. It also includes the version information for the latest release.

entries.title: Security
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the release of `androidx.security:security-state:1.1.0-alpha02`. This update includes bug fixes related to System Published SPL and System Modules SPL, ensuring the use of the 'global max' Android Security Bulletin date to support the 'Risk Based Update System' (RBUS).

entries.title: Sqlite
entries.summary: This update introduces version 2.7.0-alpha01, including asynchronous APIs for web targets, a new `sqlite-web` artifact for web worker integration, and various bug fixes and improvements in the 2.6.x series, such as enhanced error codes and performance optimizations.

entries.title: Test Uiautomator
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the release of UI Automator 2.4.0-beta02. This release includes bug fixes, specifically addressing an issue with `UiObject2#scrollToElementOrNull`.

entries.title: Tracing
entries.summary: The tracing.md documentation was updated to reflect the release of tracing-2.0.0-alpha03. This release includes API changes, bug fixes, and details on the included commits.

entries.title: tv
entries.summary: This document details the release of androidx.tv libraries. It includes updates to the release dates and dependency versions for tv-foundation and tv-material.  Significant changes include the introduction of beta versions for tv-foundation and tv-material, and updates to the dependencies to support Kotlin 2.0. Additionally, API changes are documented, including the deprecation of `androidx.compose.ui.graphics.NativePaint` and its replacement with `android.graphics.Paint`.

entries.title: Wear Compose
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the release of Wear Compose 1.6.0-rc01. This release candidate includes no significant changes compared to the prior beta release, but marks a step towards the final 1.6.0 release.

entries.title: wear protolayout
entries.summary: This update introduces version 1.4.0-rc02 of the wear-protolayout library, released on March 11, 2026. The key change is that applications targeting API 36 and later, which use ProtoLayout Expressions to access heart rate data (e.g., `heartRateBpm`, `heartRateAccuracy`), now require the `android.health.connect.HealthPermissions.READ_HEART_RATE` permission. This change ensures user privacy and data security by requiring explicit permission to access sensitive health information.

entries.title: Wear Tiles
entries.summary: The Wear Tiles documentation was updated to reflect the latest release candidate. Version 1.6.0-rc02 was released, including the release date and a link to the commits. This update ensures developers have access to the most current information regarding the Wear Tiles library.

entries.title: Wear Watch Face Push
entries.summary: The documentation for the Wear Watch Face Push library has been updated to reflect the release candidate version 1.0.0-rc01. This includes updating the release date, dependency declarations, and version information, indicating progress towards a stable release.

entries.title: Webkit
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the `webkit.md` file, which includes updates for the AndroidX Webkit library. The changes introduce new features, API changes, and external contributions in version 1.16.0-alpha03, released on March 11, 2026. This release includes enhancements to web performance metrics, error information, BackForwardCache settings, and the addition of debug information for prefetch requests. It also includes API changes such as increasing the minimum supported SDK, removing experimental features, and migrating OutcomeReceiverCompat.

entries.title: WindowManager
entries.summary: The documentation for the WindowManager library was updated to reflect the latest releases and changes, including new features, API changes, and bug fixes in versions 1.6.0-alpha01, 1.5.1, 1.5.0, and earlier release candidates and betas.  Key changes include the addition of helper methods for constructing `WindowSizeClassSets` in a grid form, the addition of Large and XLarge `WindowSizeClass` breakpoints, expanding the calculation of `WindowMetrics` to the Application Context, providing a getter for direct access to `WindowLayoutInfo`, and introducing APIs for auto-saving and restoring embedding state.  Various bug fixes are also included.

entries.title: Jetpack Compose Glimmer
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the xr-glimmer library. The update includes the release of version 1.0.0-alpha08, incorporating API changes such as replacing `TitleChipDefaults.contentPadding` with a `ContentPadding` constant, adding `ScrollIndicatorState` to `ListState`, and introducing a new `TextStyle` called `caption` with updates to existing text styles.

entries.summary: This update reflects the latest versions of various AndroidX libraries. Notable changes include new stable releases for `activity`, `collection`, `compose`, `compose.animation`, `compose.foundation`, `compose.material`, `core`, and `credentials`, along with alpha and beta releases for several other libraries. These updates provide bug fixes, performance improvements, and new features for developers.

entries.title: Android Gradle Plugin 4.1.0 (August 2020)
entries.summary: The release notes document updates related to the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) version 4.1.0.  Changes include adjustments to Gradle and SDK Build Tools requirements, and code examples for prefab configurations.

entries.title: Create and monitor geofences
entries.summary: The example XML code snippet for registering a broadcast receiver within the `<application>` tag has been corrected. Specifically, the closing angle bracket was missing in the `android:name` attribute. This fix ensures the receiver is properly registered in the manifest file, which is crucial for the geofencing functionality to work correctly, as it allows the app to receive geofence transition events.

entries.title: Styles in Compose
entries.summary: This document introduces the new Style API for Jetpack Compose, a new way to customize UI elements, offering improved flexibility, performance, and easier design system creation compared to traditional modifiers and styling parameters. It explains the core concepts of Styles, StyleScope, and StyleState, and provides guidance on how to get started with the new API.

entries.title: Do's and don'ts with Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides guidelines, best practices, and patterns for effectively utilizing Styles in Android Compose to maintain consistency and improve code design. It outlines "Do's" and "Don'ts" for visual configurations, design systems, animations, and interaction logic when working with Styles.

entries.title: Examples with Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides examples of using Styles in Android Compose to create custom UI components, specifically focusing on button styling with various effects like hover, depth, and gradient glows, and demonstrating how to apply multiple styles and animations.

entries.title: Fundamentals of Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation introduces the concept of Styles in Jetpack Compose, explaining how to apply them directly to components, through modifiers, and by defining standalone styles for reusability, including inheritance and custom property implementation.

entries.title: Current limitations
entries.summary: This new documentation page outlines the functional limitations of the Styles API in Android Compose, covering aspects like animation support, property scoping, shapes, and interoperability with other UI elements.

entries.title: Performance benefits with Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces Compose Styles and their performance benefits. It explains how Styles optimize rendering by operating in the layout and drawing phases, reducing object overhead, and deferring allocations, leading to improved performance compared to using modifiers.

entries.title: State and animations in Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces the Styles API for managing UI changes based on interaction states like hovered, focused, and pressed, streamlining the process and reducing boilerplate code, with built-in animation support.

entries.title: Styles versus modifiers
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces the concept of Styles in Jetpack Compose, comparing and contrasting them with Modifiers, outlining their respective strengths and limitations, and providing guidance on when to choose one over the other for UI development. It explains that Styles are specialized Modifiers primarily designed for visual appearance, theming, and animation optimization, whereas Modifiers are more suitable for defining behaviors, complex layouts, and adding functionalities.

entries.title: Image keyboard support
entries.summary: The code now correctly checks the Android API level using `>=` instead of `=` for `Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.N_MR1` in both Kotlin and Java examples. This ensures the correct behavior when granting read URI permission for input content.

entries.title: Track a device's pose using ARCore for Jetpack XR
entries.summary: The documentation for device pose has been updated with code samples and clarified explanations. Specifically, code examples now use `deviceTracking` instead of `deviceTrackingMode` and provide two methods for obtaining the device pose: once or continuously via a collector. Additionally, a newline and example code was added after the `processDevicePose` function call. The note about the `HEAD_TRACKING` permission has been updated to use a URL rather than a Markdown link.

entries.title: Work with geospatial poses using ARCore for Jetpack XR
entries.summary: The documentation for the Geospatial API was updated to improve code clarity and provide better examples. The changes include code formatting improvements, updated code snippets, and additional context to aid in understanding the API's usage.

Notably, several recipes have been updated to provide better examples and instructions for common use cases. Furthermore, recipes using Scenes have been updated to reflect the new API and a new 'Material Adaptive' section has been added. These changes improve the clarity and usefulness of the navigation documentation.

This table describes fields such as record type, unit of measurement, and mandatory fields to maintain data integrity.  The table includes a list of the mandatory fields for each type to improve clarity and assist developers.

entries.title: Develop Sleep Experiences with Health Connect
entries.summary: This new documentation details how to build sleep tracking experiences within Android apps using Health Connect, covering data types, permissions, background execution, and recommended workflows for comprehensive sleep data management.

entries.title: Develop Vitals Experiences with Health Connect
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides a guide on how to build apps that manage user vitals data using Health Connect, covering permissions, read/write workflows, and best practices for data management.

entries.title: Develop Workout Experiences with Health Connect
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides a guide for developers on building workout tracking experiences using Health Connect, covering data types, background execution, permissions, workflows, and best practices for creating comprehensive workout features.

entries.title: Add exercise routes
entries.summary: The documentation for exercise routes has been updated to clarify permission handling. The note regarding permission behavior was updated to use a note block, and the guidance on checking for granted permissions has been made more explicit to ensure that apps handle scenarios where permission is lost before using any permission. These changes improve clarity and reinforce best practices for handling user permissions within the Health Connect framework.

entries.title: Track mindfulness

The changes include correcting the prompt for checking permissions and providing a new code snippet demonstrating how to read mindfulness sessions within a specified time range, including how to process the returned records. Additionally, the code for writing mindfulness sessions is included.

entries.title: Measure skin temperature

The documentation also stresses the importance of regularly checking for permissions, which is critical for ensuring that an app can use sensitive health data.

entries.title: Track sleep sessions
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to clarify the difference between subtype and associated data in sleep sessions. Associated data, unlike subtype data, is recorded independently and has its own UUID. The example code for adding sleep stages has been enhanced with complete stage definitions, including specific start and end times, and timezone offsets. Additionally, the permission check reminder was updated and the formatting of the notes section was changed.

entries.title: Track steps
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect best practices for permission handling. The guide now recommends checking permissions before use and handling scenarios where permissions are lost, which is critical for maintaining app functionality and data access.

These changes aim to improve the clarity and formatting of the documentation, as well as to improve the best practice guidance for Health Connect app developers.

entries.title: Common problems and solutions
entries.summary: This document provides a guide to common problems encountered when using the NDK and their solutions. It covers issues related to `_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64`, `minSdkVersion`, symbol resolution, atomic operations, RTTI/exceptions across library boundaries, and using mismatched prebuilt libraries.

entries.title: Common problems and solutions
entries.summary: The document explains that using `_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64` with older API levels may result in fewer functions being available. This is because `mmap64` was not available until Android 21. If the `minSdkVersion` is lower than 21, the C library does not contain an `mmap` that is compatible with `_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64`.

entries.title: Common problems and solutions
entries.summary: The document provides a solution to the problem where exceptions are not being caught or `dynamic_cast` is failing across shared library boundaries. The recommended solution is to add a key function to your types. When a type does not have a key function, its typeinfo is emitted as a weak symbol and matching type infos are merged when libraries are loaded. When loading libraries dynamically, it may not be possible for the loader to merge type infos for the loaded libraries.

It also updates Wear OS emulator pairing, adds new toolbar buttons for common actions, and provides new run configurations. Logcat receives major updates with enhanced formatting, multi-window support, view presets, and a new key-value search system for more efficient log analysis.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel

Key updates include enhancements to Logcat, integration with Firebase Crashlytics for app quality insights, live updates for Compose Preview, support for different devices in Compose Preview, recomposition rendering highlights in the Layout Inspector, and visual linting for views.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel
entries.summary: The new version of Logcat is enabled by default, improving log parsing, querying, and tracking.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel
entries.summary: Android Studio now integrates with Firebase Crashlytics, allowing developers to view and address crash data directly within the IDE. This includes highlighted code lines, stack trace navigation, and filtering options.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel
entries.summary: This integration streamlines the debugging process by bringing crash data directly into the development environment, improving app performance, and minimizing disruptions for users.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel
entries.summary: Compose Previews now update immediately as changes are made.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel
entries.summary: The `device` parameter in the Preview annotation can now be used to configure Composables for different devices.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel
entries.summary: Recompositions are highlighted in the Layout Inspector to help identify areas of the UI that are recomposing.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel
entries.summary: Android Studio now checks for visual lint issues across different screen sizes.

entries.title: Android Studio Electric Eel
entries.summary: The document mentions that the universal problems panel is available but doesn't provide enough information to create a meaningful summary.

entries.title: Android Studio Flamingo
entries.summary: This document describes the new features and improvements in Android Studio Flamingo. Key updates include enhancements to themed app icons and dynamic color previews in both Compose and XML, updates to Live Edit with manual and automatic modes, the introduction of Compose Tracing for performance analysis, new project templates, and device mirroring for streamlined development. These features aim to improve developer productivity and provide better tools for creating modern Android applications.

entries.title: Android Studio Giraffe
entries.summary: This document describes the new features and improvements introduced in Android Studio Giraffe, including Live Edit for Compose, a new UI theme, enhanced Compose Animation Preview support, the Grammatical Inflection API, an updated Device Explorer, a new Android SDK Upgrade Assistant, enhanced diagnostic tools, a Make Selected Modules toolbar button, and download information during sync.

entries.title: Android Studio Hedgehog
entries.summary: Android Studio Hedgehog introduces several new features, including integration of Android vitals data within App Quality Insights, a new Power Profiler to monitor device power consumption, an updated App Links Assistant for managing deep links, a new shortcut for Live Edit manual mode, new Compose Multipreview templates and Gallery mode, improved Compose state information in the debugger, and the ability to mirror physical devices within Android Studio.

entries.title: Android Studio Koala Feature Drop
entries.summary: This update introduces code block formatting changes within the 'as-koala-feature-drop-release-notes.md' document. Specifically, it updates the code syntax to include correct code block identifiers, which improves readability and code parsing.

entries.title: Android Studio Koala
entries.summary: Android Studio Koala release notes were updated to reflect the new features and improvements. Specifically, the Gemini API template, side-by-side layout in the Running Devices window, and IntelliJ 2024.1 platform updates are highlighted. These enhancements provide developers with tools for building AI-powered apps, improved device management, and a more feature-rich development environment.

entries.title: Android Studio Ladybug Feature Drop
entries.summary: This release note details new features in Android Studio Ladybug Feature Drop, including Wear Tiles Animation Preview, Motion Editor deprecation, new Gemini-powered features, Google Play SDK Index integration enhancements, mock sensor capabilities, App Links Assistant improvements, and notifications for missing build steps in run configurations. These updates aim to enhance developer productivity, improve debugging, and streamline app development workflows.

entries.title: Android Studio Ladybug
entries.summary: This release of Android Studio Ladybug includes updates to the IntelliJ platform and support for Pixel 9 series devices. The IntelliJ update brings improvements like enhanced Kotlin stability and IDE startup efficiency.  Android Studio now includes device definitions for the Pixel 9 series, allowing developers to test their applications on virtual devices representing the new hardware, ensuring compatibility across different screen sizes and device types.

entries.title: Android Studio Meerkat Feature Drop
entries.summary: This document describes new features in Android Studio Meerkat Feature Drop. These include improvements to themed icon previews, changes to Android Studio configuration directories, a new Prompt Library feature for Gemini, and the deprecation of the Motion Editor in favor of Jetpack Compose for animations.

entries.title: Android Studio Meerkat
entries.summary: Android Studio Meerkat introduces several enhancements and new features. Key improvements include Compose Preview enhancements (smoother zoom, collapsible groups, and view modes), streamlined KMP Shared Module integration, an improved UX for device management, new Gemini features, and updates to the Build menu and actions. Additionally, the release incorporates Google Play SDK Insights for deprecated SDK warnings, helping developers stay informed about SDK deprecations and alternatives.

entries.title: Android Studio Narwhal 3 Feature Drop
entries.summary: This document outlines new features in Android Studio Narwhal 3 Feature Drop. Key changes include dynamic resizing for Compose previews, a setting to display build files under corresponding modules in the Android view, the inclusion of Play Policy Insights as lint checks, and the ability to generate and restore app backups for testing purposes.

entries.title: Android Studio Narwhal 4 Feature Drop
entries.summary: This update to Android Studio Narwhal 4 Feature Drop includes new features and improvements. Key changes include a new setting to open projects in Project view by default, Gemini in Android Studio updates (stable Agent Mode, Ask mode renaming, and image support), Android SDK Upgrade Assistant support for Android 16, and enhanced Watch Face Format support with direct XML editing, code completion, and deployment capabilities.

entries.title: Android Studio Narwhal Feature Drop
entries.summary: This document describes the new features and improvements in the Android Studio Narwhal Feature Drop, including enhancements to Gemini in Agent Mode, new features for XR development (embedded XR Emulator, XR project template, and embedded Layout Inspector), support for 16 KB page sizes, Compose preview improvements, and the use of K2 Kotlin compiler by default. Additionally, it mentions the availability of Partner Device Labs and the Compose preview picker.

entries.title: Android Studio Narwhal
entries.summary: Android Studio Narwhal introduces new features including Gemini for businesses, Studio Labs for experimenting with AI features, and enhancements to the Embedded Layout Inspector component tree. The Layout Inspector improvements include horizontal scrolling, automatic scrolling on selection, and refined relationship visualization within the component tree, aimed at providing a more efficient debugging experience.

entries.title: Android Studio Otter 2 Feature Drop (December 2025)
entries.summary: This document describes new features in Android Studio Otter 2 Feature Drop. Key changes include updating the title and URL to use a direct link, refining the Android Knowledge Base's description, and updating the communications opt-in image URL.

entries.title: Android Studio Otter
entries.summary: This document describes the new backup and sync feature in Android Studio Otter. Users can now back up their Android Studio settings, such as keymaps and editor settings, to cloud storage using either their Google or JetBrains account. This feature allows users to sync their settings across different installations of Android Studio or IntelliJ products.

entries.title: Android Studio release names
entries.summary: The document has been updated to clarify the distinction between Merge and Feature Drop releases. Merge releases, which initiate a new animal cycle, incorporate the latest IntelliJ updates and may include limited Android-Studio-specific features, while Feature Drop releases, following the first release in the cycle, introduce new Android Studio features and bug fixes. The update provides clearer definitions and examples of these release types, enhancing understanding of the Android Studio release strategy.

entries.title: Report a bug
entries.summary: The documentation for reporting bugs in Android Studio has been updated with clearer instructions, including guidance on how to avoid reporting already fixed bugs and how to include necessary diagnostic information such as CPU profiles and memory usage reports. Formatting and wording were updated for clarity. The documentation also includes steps to generate thread dumps for debugging.

entries.title: Build and run your app
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to use the `[!NOTE]` callout style instead of the `| **Note:**` style for notes. This change improves the visual clarity and consistency of the documentation.

entries.title: Advanced emulator usage
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to improve clarity and organization. It now includes a more descriptive introduction to advanced emulator usage, outlining the features covered on the current page and referencing other pages within the section that cover more advanced use cases that require the terminal. The text has been slightly reworded for better readability and to ensure the links work.

entries.title: Android Device Streaming, powered by Firebase
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Device Streaming has been updated to include the use of `[!NOTE]` callout blocks for important information and an updated list of available devices. These changes enhance the readability and clarity of the documentation.

entries.title: Configure hardware acceleration for the Android Emulator
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the deprecation of certain graphics acceleration modes and the sunset of the Android Emulator hypervisor driver (AEHD) on Windows, recommending users transition to Windows Hypervisor Platform (WHPX). Several notes and warnings have been updated to use the correct formatting and the usage of specific command-line options are clarified.

entries.title: Install and add files
entries.summary: The documentation for installing and adding files to the Android emulator was updated. The changes include more explicit language about the use case of installing APKs and adding files. The text was also slightly reworded for clarity and conciseness, and some formatting changes were made. This is important to ensure users have clear instructions for sideloading apps and transferring files to the emulator for testing and development purposes.

entries.title: Launch the emulator in a separate window
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to clarify that the Android Emulator runs inside Android Studio by default and why this is beneficial. It explains how to launch the emulator in a separate window to access certain features. This change provides a clearer understanding of the default behavior and how to change it for specific use cases.

entries.title: Launch the emulator without launching an app
entries.summary: The documentation clarifies the scenario where launching the emulator without an app is useful, such as testing Bluetooth interactions. It also includes minor wording adjustments for better readability.

entries.title: Record the screen
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify how to start and stop screen recording in the Android Emulator. It provides more explicit instructions on using the Record and Playback tab within the Extended Controls and updated the command line example to match the new text.

entries.title: Snapshots
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify and expand upon existing information about emulator snapshots. Specifically, the steps for saving Quick Boot snapshots were clarified and a note was added about skipping automatic saves. Also, the text was formatted for readability, and minor clarifications were made throughout the document.

entries.title: Take screenshots
entries.summary: The documentation for taking screenshots in the Android Emulator was updated. The changes include clarification on how to use the 'Take Screenshot' dialog, detailing the options available for editing, saving, and the naming convention of the generated PNG files. Additionally, the formatting of the command-line options was updated.

entries.title: Troubleshoot known issues with Android Emulator
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to improve clarity, fix formatting issues, and provide more accurate troubleshooting advice. Notably, it changes the initial introductory paragraph, updates troubleshooting steps for slow emulator performance, and adds better formatting.

entries.title: Camera support
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Emulator camera functionality was updated. The changes include improvements in formatting and wording, and minor modifications to the instructions for using the virtual scene camera and macros.

entries.title: Wi-Fi
entries.summary: The documentation for emulator-wifi was updated. The changes remove the introductory heading "Wi-Fi".

entries.title: Run apps on the Android Emulator
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of the Android Emulator, explaining its advantages, setup, and usage for testing Android applications. It also covers system requirements, AVD creation, and app deployment on the emulator.

entries.title: Test on multiple screen sizes with the resizable emulator
entries.summary: The documentation for the resizable emulator has been updated. The note about the emulator no longer being experimental is now emphasized, and there are some minor wording adjustments for clarity. This is important as it indicates the feature is now stable and ready for wider use.

entries.title: Service integrations in Android Studio
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Studio service integrations has been updated to improve readability and formatting. The changes include improvements in text layout, link formatting, and table formatting.

entries.title: Deprecated service integrations
entries.summary: The documentation details deprecated Android Studio service integrations. Specifically, it lists integrations such as Meerkat, Cloud Source Repositories project import, and App Engine support, which are scheduled for removal starting in the Narwhal Feature Drop. Additionally, it mentions the deprecation of "Run with Firebase Test Lab", slated for removal in Android Studio "O" Feature Drop. These services are no longer supported in newer versions of Android Studio.

entries.title: Test your app
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to improve readability and clarity. The text has been rephrased for better flow and formatting. Additionally, minor adjustments to the links were made.

entries.title: Advanced test setup
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to improve clarity and formatting. The changes include improvements in text formatting and corrected links and code samples. The content also updated to newer Gradle APIs.

entries.title: Test from the command line
entries.summary: The documentation updates include minor content and formatting adjustments.  Specifically, it updates the language, formatting, and the use of markdown elements to improve readability, along with minor clarifications to the text.

entries.title: Other testing tools
entries.summary: The document provides descriptions of three testing tools: Espresso Test Recorder, App Crawler, and Monkey. These tools offer various approaches to testing Android applications, from interactive recording to automated user action testing and stress testing. These tools help developers to automate the process of testing their apps.

entries.title: App Crawler
entries.summary: This document describes the App Crawler tool, which is used for automatically testing Android apps. The changes mostly refine the existing content by improving formatting, clarifying language, and updating links.

entries.title: Test in Android Studio
entries.summary: This document was updated to use a more modern syntax for emphasis, fix broken links, and improve clarity. It also adds a note to clarify the inclusion of Android framework APIs in local unit tests.

entries.title: Troubleshoot Android Studio
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify and correct information related to high-density displays on Windows and Linux, including scaling factor settings, blurry/pixelated element issues, and incorrect element sizing. Also, the document was updated to use more correct paths for Windows directories.

entries.title: Write your app
entries.summary: The documentation was updated with minor text adjustments. It changed the wording in several sections and included minor formatting adjustments. There are no functional changes.

entries.title: Add app resources
entries.summary: This document provides a comprehensive guide on managing Android app resources, including adding, organizing, and merging them. It has been updated with detailed instructions on adding XML resources, resource directories, changing resource locations, and understanding the resource merging process, along with links to relevant documentation.

entries.title: Create WebP images
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of WebP image format, its benefits, and how to convert images to and from WebP format using Android Studio. The changes include improved formatting, more informative descriptions, and clearer steps for image conversion, enhancing the user experience.

entries.title: Connect to Firebase
entries.summary: This document provides instructions on how to connect an Android app to Firebase using the Assistant tool window in Android Studio. The changes include minor formatting adjustments, such as line breaks for better readability, and some rewording for clarity. These changes aim to improve the presentation and clarity of the instructions for developers integrating Firebase services into their apps.

entries.title: Develop a UI with Views
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the recommendation of building UI with Compose instead of Views. Minor formatting changes and clarifications have also been added for better readability. The document also includes updates to the buttons in the layout editor toolbar.

entries.title: Build animation with the Motion Editor
entries.summary: The Motion Editor documentation has been updated to reflect its deprecation in Android Studio Meerkat. A deprecation warning has been added at the beginning of the document.

entries.title: Manage your app's UI resources with Resource Manager
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to improve readability and clarity. Changes include formatting adjustments, more descriptive text, and minor updates to the import process description.  The addition of the 'drawable' drag and drop feature descriptions are particularly important to help users to understand the resource manager functionality better.

entries.title: Tools attributes reference
entries.summary: This document describes the `tools` namespace attributes in Android Studio for design-time and compile-time features. It details attributes for lint suppression, API level targeting, locale specification, design-time view customization, and layout preview behavior within the Android Studio layout editor. The document has been updated with minor text improvements and formatting changes.

entries.title: Generic System Image releases
entries.summary: This document introduces Android 17 GSI releases, including links to download images and associated terms and conditions. It provides details on the binaries, supported devices, and license agreements for usage. It is a new section to the documentation.

entries.title: Releases
entries.summary: This document was updated to include information about Android 15. Specifically, apps using the Android for Cars App Library must update to version 1.7.0-rc01 or later to prevent crashes on Android 15 due to secured background activity launch changes when permission dialogs are triggered. The document also reiterates the importance of testing apps for proper rendering due to window inset changes in Android 15, and provides a link to relevant documentation.

entries.title: Features and APIs
entries.summary: This document introduces the UWB Downlink-TDoA API for Android 17. It details how to initialize the Ranging Manager, verify device capabilities, start a DL-TDoA session, and configure parameters using both Kotlin and Java code examples. It also provides guidance on Out-of-Band (OOB) configurations for Wi-Fi and BLE, as well as alternative parameter building methods.

entries.title: Android Bench
entries.summary: This new documentation introduces "Android Bench," a benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the capabilities of LLMs in Android development, addressing challenges not covered by existing benchmarks.

entries.title: Android Bench
entries.summary: This new documentation, "methodology.md," details the Android Bench benchmark, outlining its goals, methodology, and composition for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs) in Android development. It aims to encourage LLM improvements and empower developers with AI assistance.

entries.title: Components
entries.summary: The documentation for AI glasses components has been updated to provide a more detailed explanation of the optimizations made for transparent displays. The changes include specifying that surfaces use transparency by changing the surface color to black to make it transparent, adding details on outlines, highlights, shapes, typography, and iconography. This helps developers understand the specific design considerations for building user interfaces on AI glasses, leading to better visual experiences.

entries.title: Get started
entries.summary: This document provides a guide to building AI Glasses apps using the Jetpack Compose Glimmer UI framework.  The changes primarily involve minor wording adjustments for clarity, formatting, and improved image alt text. The core content and guidance on design principles, user journeys, audio flows, and system UI remain consistent.

entries.title: Range between devices
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to include information about the UWB Downlink-TDoA API. This addition provides a link to the Android 17 features page, offering developers details about the new API for utilizing UWB technology to measure distance and location with improved accuracy.

entries.title: Develop spatial UI with Jetpack Compose for XR
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the addition of `PlanarEmbeddedSubspace` and changes to the `Subspace` composable.  `PlanarEmbeddedSubspace` allows for the integration of 3D content within a 2D layout, offering greater flexibility. Also, the documentation clarifies `Subspace` behavior and the importance of using subspace composables within the appropriate context. These changes provide developers with additional options for spatial UI design and clarify existing usage patterns.

entries.title: Play Games PC samples
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces code samples designed to help developers quickly integrate Play Games PC features into their games. It includes samples for Play Billing integration and demonstrates the use of the Play PC SDK within a Unity project.

entries.title: Subscription lifecycle
entries.summary: The documentation for subscriptions.md was updated to replace all instances of "**Note:**" and "**Warning:**" with blockquotes using "[!NOTE]" and "[!WARNING]" respectively. This change improves the visual presentation and readability of the document by using a more structured format for important notes and warnings. Additionally, there was a minor change in the cancellation type from `USER_REQUESTED_STOP_RENEWALS` to `USER_REQUESTED_STOP_RENEWALS`.

entries.title: Manage subscriptions and one-time purchases
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the change of `cancellationType` value from `USER_REQUESTED_STOP_RENEWAL` to `USER_REQUESTED_STOP_RENEWALS` when cancelling a subscription. Additionally, the documentation now uses the NOTE formatting style. Also, clarification has been added regarding subscription revoking via API calls, especially for the installment subscriptions.

entries.title: Implement Sign in with Google
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides a guide on how to implement Sign in with Google using the Credential Manager on Android, covering steps from adding dependencies to handling sign-out.

entries.title: About Sign in with Google
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of integrating Sign in with Google using Credential Manager. It highlights the benefits, user interface guidelines, and prerequisites for implementing the feature, including version compatibility and links to relevant documentation.

entries.title: About the migration from legacy Google Sign-In
entries.summary: This document outlines the migration from legacy Google Sign-In for Android to the Android Credential Manager and AuthorizationClient API. The guide highlights the benefits of using Credential Manager, including a unified API, support for the Sign in with Google button, and streamlined sign-in flows. It also explains the differences between the legacy approach and the updated Credential Manager implementation, particularly in how authentication and authorization are handled.

entries.title: Android Mobile App Developer Tools - Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation introduces 'Android Bench', an official benchmark for Large Language Models (LLMs) used in Android development, replacing the previous Google I/O announcement. This change provides developers with a dedicated resource for evaluating and comparing LLMs, which is critical for developers looking to integrate AI into their Android applications.

entries.title: navigation3
entries.summary: Version 1.1.0-alpha04 of androidx.navigation3 has been released. This release includes a new suspending callback `onRemoved` in the `OverlayScene` interface, which is invoked before the overlay scene is removed from composition.  Additionally, the `SceneStrategy` interface has been refactored with a new `SceneDecoratorStrategy` interface to manage scene decoration, along with new APIs for `NavDisplay` and `rememberSceneState`.

entries.title: Android Studio Nightly
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the latest nightly build. Specifically, the download links and version numbers were updated from 2026-03-05 to 2026-03-06.

entries.title: Updates to non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 10
entries.summary: This document outlines changes to non-SDK interface restrictions in Android 10. Key updates include renaming the lists of restricted interfaces based on the maximum target SDK version, adding code annotations for non-SDK interfaces, and modifications to the ADB commands for managing access to these interfaces. These changes aim to improve app stability and compatibility by encouraging the use of public SDK alternatives.

entries.title: Set up the 12L SDK
entries.summary: The documentation for setting up the 12L SDK has been updated with minor formatting changes and rewording for clarity. These changes include adjustments to spacing, line breaks, and emphasis, ensuring better readability of the instructions for developers.

entries.title: Set up the Android 12 SDK
entries.summary: This document provides instructions on setting up the Android 12 SDK in Android Studio, including selecting the correct SDK Platform and Build-Tools versions. It also explains how to update the app's build configuration to target Android 12 and links to resources about behavior changes and new APIs.

entries.title: Android 12 features and changes list
entries.summary: This document provides a summary of features and behavior changes in Android 12 that impact app developers. The content is presented in a table format, categorizing changes by areas like Activity lifecycle, Camera, Connectivity, Core functionality, and others. Each entry includes the category, type of change, and a description with a link to more detailed documentation.

entries.title: Android 13 features and changes list
entries.summary: This document outlines changes for Android 13 affecting app developers, detailing new features, API updates, and behavior changes across various categories like camera, connectivity, developer tools, graphics, media, and more.  The updates cover a wide range, from deprecated APIs to performance improvements and new user experience features.

entries.title: Schema for app metadata bundles
entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to the app metadata schema in Android 14. The primary change involves replacing all instances of the "Note:" style with the "NOTE:" callouts, and "Important:" to "IMPORTANT:" callouts, to improve readability. These changes do not affect the functionality of the schema itself but enhance the clarity of the documentation.

entries.title: Set up the Android 14 SDK
entries.summary: This document provides instructions on setting up the Android 14 SDK in Android Studio, including selecting the correct packages and updating the app's build configuration. It also provides links to further documentation regarding behavior changes and new features in Android 14. The changes involve formatting and minor wording updates for clarity.

entries.title: Android 14 features and changes list
entries.summary: This document outlines changes for Android 14, including new features and behavior changes that affect app developers, organized by category and type. It provides links to detailed documentation for each change.

entries.title: Factory images for Google Pixel
entries.summary: The document was updated to include the Pixel 6a as a supported device for downloading the Android 17 factory system image. Additionally, it appears the terms and conditions and download links were updated for the specific factory images.

entries.title: Features and APIs
entries.summary: The documentation for Android 17 features has been updated. The API diff report link in the document was updated from 'c-dp1' to 'c-beta2'.

entries.title: Behavior changes: apps targeting API level 28+
entries.summary: The documentation updates two "Note" sections. The update replaces the original "Note:" with a new format '> [!NOTE]' in the markdown. This change improves the visual formatting of the document, making the notes more distinct from the surrounding text, which enhances readability.

entries.title: Configure your build
entries.summary: The Android Gradle plugin versions for both application and library projects have been updated from 9.0.0 to 9.1.0 in the `build.md` documentation. This change likely reflects an update to the Android Gradle Plugin, which might contain new features, bug fixes, or performance improvements that are important for Android development.

entries.title: Configure build variants
entries.summary: The documentation for build variants was updated. The changes include an update to the applicationIdSuffix link to reflect the new API version 9.1. The update is relevant for developers using the API.

entries.title: Modern Android App Architecture

entries.title: UI Design
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the evolving Android ecosystem. Specifically, the "Adapt to multiple form factors" section was changed to "Enhance your app's productivity", emphasizing support for desktop windowing and input methods. Additionally, some content and links were updated to reflect changes in available design resources and guidance.

entries.title: Keyboard interaction
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides guidelines for implementing keyboard navigation and keyboard shortcuts in Android desktop applications, focusing on enhancing user efficiency and accessibility.

entries.title: System bars
entries.summary: This new documentation introduces the system UI changes for desktop-like Android environments, including the taskbar and window header bar, when an extended or connected display is available.

entries.title: Migrating from Firebase JobDispatcher to WorkManager
entries.summary: The changes update the code examples in the Firebase documentation related to WorkManager. Specifically, they correct typos and update the builder syntax for creating work requests, ensuring code accuracy and functionality.

entries.title: Use bubbles to let users participate in conversations
entries.summary: The documentation for Android bubbles was updated. The section header "The notification Bubble API" was changed to "The bubble API". This is a minor clarification in the title.

entries.title: Start an Activity from a Notification
entries.summary: The documentation for starting an Activity from a notification was updated to use more modern links and references to Android developer documentation.  Additionally, code examples were updated to utilize Kotlin and Java best practices and point to the correct documentation.

entries.title: Storing and Searching for Data
entries.summary: The documentation for the Android search feature has been updated to fix minor code issues. The changes include correcting a conditional statement in the Kotlin code, and in the Java code. Also, a type issue has been corrected for selection arguments.

entries.title: Detect common gestures
entries.summary: The code example in detector.md, which demonstrates handling touch events using `onTouchEvent`, has been updated.  The changes replace the hyphens used to separate cases in the `when` statement with arrows, and replaces multiple `>` symbols with the correct syntax to use a case for `MotionEvent.ACTION_OUTSIDE` and default `else` in Kotlin, thus improving readability and correctness of the code sample.

entries.title: Develop with the Jetpack XR SDK
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the change from `Volume` to `SceneCoreEntity` composable for placing 3D models and other entities in the spatial layout using Jetpack Compose for XR. This change provides a more specific and accurate API for developers working with XR applications and using SceneCore entities.

entries.title: Android Game Development Extension for Visual Studio
entries.summary: This update includes various formatting adjustments, such as corrected links, a new video link, and minor text refinements. Furthermore, it updates the download button and includes an adjustment in the license terms.

entries.title: Release notes

entries.title: Android Games Development Kit

The changes include improvements in formatting, additions of download instructions, links, and the introduction of new sections like Android Game Development Extension for Visual Studio (AGDE). The update also contains the full legal terms and conditions for AGDK use, including export restrictions and license agreements, clarifying the legal aspects of using the AGDK.

entries.title: Native and proprietary engines
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect improvements in formatting and clarity.  The most notable change is the conversion of standard markdown notes to a more structured callout format. The provided example of determining the highest supported Vulkan API version was also updated. These changes aim to enhance readability and improve the overall presentation of information for developers.

entries.title: Android game development
entries.summary: This update to the Android game development documentation includes a new section detailing the terms and conditions for using the Android SDK, alongside an updated download link for the Android Game Development Extension for Windows.

entries.title: Google Play Games
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect an announcement regarding the Level Up program. The document specifies that there are no immediate changes to the current program or its benefits as of March 4, 2026.  It also slightly adjusted the content of the deadline milestones by removing an unneeded reference to using the Play Games Services v2 SDK.

entries.title: Achievements for Android games
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to use the `> [!NOTE]` syntax for the notes and code snippets were changed to not have the `scdoc` and `transact-sql` tags. These changes improve the readability and consistency of the document.

entries.title: Android game development
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the new name of the LevelUp program. The link to the Level Up program was also updated. This ensures the documentation is current and provides the correct information for developers integrating with Google Play Games Services.

entries.title: Events: resetMultipleForAllPlayers
entries.summary: The documentation for the `resetMultipleForAllPlayers` API has been updated. The primary change involves correcting a typo in the request body example by changing `;even}t_ids` to `event_ids`. This clarifies the expected structure of the JSON payload that must be sent in the request.

entries.title: Gemini in Android Studio - Android Developers
entries.summary: gemini-in-android.md documentation has been updated.

entries.title: Compatibility framework tools
entries.summary: The documentation updates the format of the notes to use the `> [!NOTE]` syntax for better readability and consistency. Also, the documentation clarifies the ability to toggle changes using the developer options in different build types.

entries.title: Activity state changes
entries.summary: This document describes how activities transition between different states in Android. It covers common scenarios like configuration changes, multi-window mode, activities appearing in the foreground, back button presses, and the system killing app processes. The document also provides links to other relevant documentation.

entries.title: Add support for the predictive back gesture
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to include the use of callout blocks, and the text emphasizes the importance of implementing predictive back navigation. Also, it clarified that system animations are no longer hidden behind developer options on Android 15 and later.

entries.title: Animations Recipe
entries.summary: The documentation for animations now includes a link to the full recipe on GitHub for the example provided. This change allows developers to directly access and explore the code, making it easier to understand and implement the animation techniques demonstrated.

entries.title: Basic Recipe
entries.summary: The documentation for the basic navigation recipe was updated. A link to the full recipe on GitHub has been added, allowing developers to explore the complete code implementation.

entries.title: Bottom Sheet Recipe
entries.summary: The documentation for bottom sheets in Android Navigation has been updated to include a link to the full recipe on GitHub. This change allows developers to directly access and explore the complete code implementation for bottom sheets, facilitating easier understanding and practical application of the concept.

entries.title: Conditional Navigation Recipe
entries.summary: This document introduces a conditional navigation recipe, detailing how to implement navigation logic based on user authentication status. It provides a Navigator class to manage navigation events, redirecting users to a login screen if they attempt to access restricted destinations while not logged in. The changes also include the addition of the full recipe code.

entries.title: Integrate asset delivery (Kotlin and Java)

It also updates code examples to use a more accurate `asset-pack-name` and removes unnecessary translation tags.

entries.title: Integrate asset delivery (Native)
entries.summary: This document was updated to clarify and correct file paths and remove unnecessary `<var translate="no">` tags, as well as updating the download link for the Play Core Native SDK to use a button and to update the SDK download link.

entries.title: Integrate Continue Watching using REST API

entries.title: Overview of Play Feature Delivery
entries.summary: The documentation updates include minor adjustments to Gradle plugin descriptions, clarifications on resource URI generation, and enhancements to manifest attribute explanations, including the correct file path for string resources and a style change for the note section.

entries.title: Support in-app updates (Kotlin or Java)
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with minor changes including updated links, formatting adjustments, and the addition of callout blocks (Note and Caution) for improved clarity and readability. These changes do not introduce new functionality but enhance the presentation of existing content.

entries.title: In-app install prompts
entries.summary: The documentation for in-app install prompts has been updated. The changes remove whitespace and formatting from the beginning of the document.

entries.title: Manage contact storage locations

These changes enhance readability and clarify important information regarding contact creation and account management, particularly when dealing with cloud and local accounts.

entries.title: Integrate single tap passkey creation and sign-in with biometric prompts
entries.summary: The documentation updates describe how to integrate single-tap biometric authentication for passkey creation and sign-in flows. It provides code snippets for setting up BiometricPromptData and handling credential entry selection, including important considerations regarding device configuration and the use of crypto objects. The updates clarify how to integrate biometric authentication with the credential manager, which enhances security and user experience.

entries.title: Learn Kotlin for Android
entries.summary: The changes in this document involve updating the links to use HTTPS and removing the list of the links in the beginning. The markdown is also improved by adding the spacing in the beginning.

entries.title: Creativity  |  Gallery  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The navigation links in the document have been updated.  The "Android Developers" and "UI Design" links were removed, and the "Home" link was added. This change reorganizes the navigation for better clarity and usability.

entries.title: Productivity  |  Gallery  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The documentation's navigation links were updated. The "Android Developers" and "UI Design" links were removed, and the "Home" link was added. The "Design & Plan" link was also moved. These changes streamline the navigation within the documentation.

entries.title: Reading  |  Gallery  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: The navigation links in the documentation were updated. Specifically, the top-level link to Android Developers was changed to 'Home', and the order of the links was adjusted. This ensures the documentation's navigation is accurate and reflects the current Android developer portal structure, improving user experience and ease of navigation.

entries.title: Configuration options
entries.summary: The documentation underwent several updates, primarily involving formatting improvements and the incorporation of "NOTE" callouts using the "[!NOTE]" syntax. These changes enhance readability and highlight important information within the document.

entries.title: Transform output
entries.summary: The documentation for transforming CameraX output was updated. The changes include correcting a link to the `Matrix.mapPoints()` method, ensuring accuracy in the documentation's references.

entries.title: Extend your media app to Android for Cars
entries.summary: The documentation updates include changes to the formatting of notes and important notices using the `>` notation. Additionally, the documentation now uses the `MediaSession` class, which takes any class that implements the `Player` interface, as can the UI. These changes improve the readability and clarify the steps needed to develop media apps for cars.

entries.title: Manage and play content
entries.summary: The code example in the documentation was updated with a new link to the source code. This update points to the latest source code on Github, ensuring the example code is up-to-date.

Additionally, the documentation now uses the "IMPORTANT" and "NOTE" callout boxes and clarifies the asynchronous nature of the `start` method and how to monitor the transformation progress.

entries.title: Frame rate
entries.summary: The documentation for frame rate optimization has been updated. The changes replace the markdown for an important note. The update rewrites the markdown for an important note related to the `setFrameRate()` function, which now uses a more visually distinct formatting style. This change highlights the scheduler's behavior and the lack of guarantees in setting frame rates, improving clarity for developers.

entries.title: Android Vulkan Profiles (AVP)
entries.summary: This document introduces the Android Vulkan Profiles (AVP), which provide a reliable set of Vulkan features for Android developers, replacing the previous Android Baseline Profiles (ABP). The document details the release of AVP 2025, highlighting new features and improvements compared to earlier profiles, such as AVP 2021 and 2022, and explains the benefits for developers looking to target a wide range of Android devices.

entries.title: Neural Networks API
entries.summary: This update modifies the formatting of the documentation by changing the warning, note and tip sections to use the markdown alert syntax.  Additionally, the table of supported operations has been updated to include links to the relevant documentation.

entries.title: Audit access to data
entries.summary: The documentation updates the formatting of notes from using bold text to the `> [!NOTE]` callout style, and makes minor wording adjustments for clarity. These changes improve the document's visual presentation and readability without altering the technical content.

entries.title: Support Direct Boot mode
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to replace some bracketed words with hyperlinks and updated some method references with hyperlinks. It also corrected a minor grammatical error.

entries.title: Local network permission
entries.summary: This document has been significantly expanded to provide detailed guidance on the ACCESS_LOCAL_NETWORK permission introduced in Android 17. It explains how the permission works, including the different approaches (using privacy-preserving pickers or requesting runtime permission), split permission model, and error handling. It also includes the Android 16 guidance.

entries.title: How to add the SafetyNet API dependency
entries.summary: The document was updated to reflect the deprecation of the SafetyNet Attestation and reCAPTCHA APIs in favor of the Play Integrity API and reCAPTCHA respectively. entries.title: Android Protected Confirmation
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Protected Confirmation has been updated with several changes.  Specifically, the documentation has been updated to use the markdown syntax for cautions and notes, replacing the previous table-based approach. This improves the visual presentation and readability of the documentation.

entries.title: Verify hardware-backed key pairs with key attestation
entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to Android Key Attestation. It includes a new root certificate for key attestation, and also clarifies the process of verifying hardware-backed key pairs, emphasizing the importance of checking certificate revocation, and the correct order of extension data extraction.

entries.title: Configure on-device developer options
entries.summary: The documentation for developer options has been updated with a note highlighting that the `adb` demo mode commands may not work on all devices. This is important for developers to be aware of when using these commands for testing and debugging.

entries.title: Debug your layout with Layout Inspector
entries.summary: The documentation for the Layout Inspector has been updated to reflect the deprecation and removal of the 3D mode feature in Android Studio Panda 2 and later versions. The update adds a note clarifying the removal of the 3D mode.

entries.title: Android Studio Nightly

entries.title: Release notes for Android Studio preview
entries.summary: This document updates the Android Studio and Gradle plugin versioning information, and describes new features in Android Studio Panda 3 including the ability to identify state variables that triggered recomposition in the Layout Inspector. It also updates a link.

entries.title: Android Studio 3.0 (October 2017)
entries.summary: Android Studio 3.0 release notes have been updated to include a link to a video overview of the release and a minor text adjustment. Additionally, the documentation now references D8 compiler, providing instructions on how to enable it, which leads to faster compilation and smaller .dex files.  Google Maven repository is now included by default in new projects, and instructions are provided for updating existing projects.

entries.title: Android Studio 3.1 (March 2018)
entries.summary: This update to the Android Studio 3.1 release notes includes minor revisions. The primary change involves adding a video link related to the release and updating the release version. Other changes include correcting a code snippet and minor wording adjustments throughout the document. These updates provide additional resources and ensure clarity in the documentation.

entries.title: Android Studio 3.4 (April 2019)
entries.summary: The release notes now include a link to a YouTube video providing a visual overview of the changes and improvements in Android Studio 3.4.0. This video helps users to quickly understand the key features and enhancements.

entries.title: Android Studio 3.5 (August 2019)
entries.summary: A video link has been added to the release notes, providing users with a visual overview of the Android Studio 3.5.0 release. This addition allows for easier consumption of information related to the release.

entries.title: Android Studio 3.6 (February 2020)

entries.title: Android Studio Iguana
entries.summary: The release notes document was updated to include a visual change for the 'Note' callout box. The markdown for the note was updated, and the image was updated for the App Quality Insights feature.

entries.title: Add multi-density vector graphics
entries.summary: This document describes the Vector Asset Studio tool in Android Studio, which helps developers add and manage vector drawables (SVG, PSD, and Material Icons) in their Android projects. entries.title: AAPT2

entries.title: apksigner

entries.title: avdmanager
entries.summary: The documentation for `avdmanager` has been updated to improve readability and clarity. The text was also updated to consistently use backticks for code and inline variables.

entries.title: bundletool
entries.summary: The documentation for `bundletool` was updated to improve readability and formatting, including using a note instead of a general remark, and clarifying instructions for building and testing app bundles, as well as providing more detailed descriptions of the command-line flags.

entries.title: d8
entries.summary: The documentation for d8, the command-line tool for converting Java bytecode to DEX bytecode, has been updated. entries.title: dmtracedump
entries.summary: The documentation for `dmtracedump` has been updated to improve readability and clarity. Changes include rewording sentences, formatting table contents and providing better explanations for the options and output of the tool.

entries.title: dumpsys

entries.title: Jetifier
entries.summary: The documentation for Jetifier has been updated to improve clarity and formatting. The updates provide a better user experience by enhancing readability and understanding of the tool's usage and advanced features.

entries.title: perfetto
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of the `perfetto` tool, which is used to collect performance information from Android devices. It describes how to use the tool via ADB, detailing its modes, general options, and syntax for both light and normal modes.

entries.title: R8 retrace
entries.summary: The documentation for R8 retrace has been updated to use markdown-style formatting for notes and option tables. The formatting changes enhance readability.

entries.title: Guide to app architecture
entries.summary: This document introduces the concept of app architecture, emphasizing the importance of a well-defined structure for building scalable and maintainable Android applications. The changes include minor wording adjustments, clarifications, and the addition of a note on diagram dependencies. The document also expands on topics such as app composition, common architectural principles (separation of concerns, adaptive layouts, and unidirectional data flow), and recommended app architecture.

entries.title: Data layer
entries.summary: This document introduces improvements in the data layer documentation, clarifying the role of the data layer, its architecture, and best practices. It emphasizes the importance of data immutability, dependency injection, and naming conventions for repositories and data sources. The updates also cover multiple levels of repositories and the dependency graphs.

entries.title: Build an offline-first app
entries.summary: This document introduces offline-first app development, emphasizing the importance of data layer design. It details how to handle reads and writes, manage local and network data sources, and implement error handling strategies in an offline-first architecture.

entries.title: Domain layer
entries.summary: The documentation for the domain layer has been updated with minor changes. The document now includes an updated image, a video, improvements in formatting, and updates to the naming conventions.

entries.title: App architecture
entries.summary: The documentation introduces a video on App Architecture and expands on existing content with a video link. This addition provides users with a video resource for further learning and understanding of app architecture concepts.

entries.title: Recommendations for Android architecture
entries.summary: This document provides recommendations for Android architecture, emphasizing best practices for improving app quality, robustness, scalability, maintainability, and testability. The changes include formatting updates, clarification of existing recommendations, and minor content adjustments, particularly around the use of layered architecture and UI layer best practices.

entries.title: UI layer
entries.summary: The documentation for the UI layer was updated to include a video link, a note regarding the application of the recommendations, and improved formatting and clarity. The document also added an important key point about data ownership.

entries.title: UI events
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on handling UI events in Android applications. It explains how to handle user events and ViewModel events, emphasizing the importance of UI state updates and unidirectional data flow. The document includes examples and best practices for managing events within the UI layer, including specific considerations for RecyclerViews and naming conventions.

entries.title: UI State production
entries.summary: The documentation for UI state production has been updated to clarify the distinction between events and state, the state production pipeline, and best practices for managing state changes in Android applications. It also adds a note regarding Kotlin and Compose code examples and their applicability to equivalent Java code.

entries.title: State holders and UI state
entries.summary: This document introduces state holders within the UI layer, explaining their role in managing UI state through unidirectional data flow. It clarifies the different types of UI state, logic, and how to choose an appropriate state holder implementation (ViewModel or plain class). Additional a video link was added.

entries.title: Dynamic System Updates (DSU)
entries.summary: This document describes Dynamic System Updates (DSU), a feature introduced in Android 10 that allows users to install and boot Generic System Images (GSIs) on their devices. This update includes formatting changes, clarifications, and addition of information on unsparsing GSI images.

entries.title: UX best practices for apps on Google Play Instant
