entries.title: UX best practices for games on Google Play Instant
entries.summary: This document outlines best practices for Google Play Instant games, including changes to the deadline for Instant App availability. It guides developers on optimizing user experience and provides details on different instant experience types and key considerations for implementation.

entries.title: Migrate your instant app to support Android App Bundles
entries.summary: This document describes the migration process for existing instant apps to support Android App Bundles, as Google Play Instant is being deprecated. The changes include plugin migrations (from `com.android.feature` to `com.android.application` and `com.android.dynamic-feature`), and adjustments to the `build.gradle` and manifest files to correctly configure the app for Android App Bundles, which streamlines the distribution of optimized APKs.

entries.title: Implement cloud delivery of assets
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the deprecation of Google Play Instant, which will no longer be available after December 2025. The content has been adjusted to focus on app size optimization using cloud delivery of assets and emphasizes the use of deeplinks to redirect users to the regular app. Additionally, the formatting and wording have been slightly improved for clarity.

entries.title: Convert an existing game to an instant game
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on developing games for Google Play Instant, focusing on changes related to the deprecation of the platform. The changes include updates to reflect the December 2025 shutdown, and provide instructions on how to use deeplinks to redirect users to their regular app or game.

entries.title: Use Google Play Instant with Unity
entries.summary: This document outlines the Google Play Instant plugin for Unity, which configures Unity projects to create instant app versions of games. It describes how to download, import, and use the plugin, including features like build settings, player settings, quick deploy, and support for install workflows using install prompts and the Cookie API.

entries.title: Create an instant-enabled app bundle
entries.summary: This document outlines changes related to the deprecation of Google Play Instant. The document also includes updates regarding SDK setup, execution conditions, and project configuration for instant-enabled app bundles.

The key change is the inclusion of a warning that Google Play Instant will no longer be available after December 2025. Additionally, the document offers steps to confirm ad network SDK compatibility and how to configure an app for ads, with a strong suggestion to use deeplinks to redirect users to regular apps/games as a best practice.

entries.title: Add Google Analytics for Firebase to your instant app
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on using Google Analytics for Firebase in Google Play Instant apps. It has been updated to reflect the deprecation of Google Play Instant and provides updated links and formatting.

entries.title: Use Firebase Dynamic Links with instant apps
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the deprecation of Google Play Instant, with the service ceasing in December 2025. It also suggests using deep links to redirect users to regular apps and provides information about Firebase Dynamic Links for setting up deep linking in instant app projects.

entries.title: Provide multiple entry points into an instant experience
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the deprecation of Google Play Instant. The changes include reformatting existing text using blockquotes for notes and cautions, and slightly clarifying the examples and explanations around multiple entry points and URL paths.

entries.title: Reduce the size of your instant app
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the deprecation of Google Play Instant, with a target date of December 2025. entries.title: Instant App Common Intents
entries.summary: The documentation for Instant App Common Intents has been updated to reflect the deprecation of Google Play Instant, effective December 2025. The changes clarify that Instant Apps will no longer be published through Google Play, and developers should use deep links to direct users to their regular apps. The document also provides a table listing the common intents used by instant apps.

entries.title: Instant play games technical requirements checklist
entries.summary: This document outlines requirements for Google Play Instant games and has been updated to reflect the deprecation of Google Play Instant. Key changes include clarifying the end-of-life for Google Play Instant, emphasizing the need for game developers to redirect users to their regular apps via deeplinks, and providing updated requirements and guidelines. Additionally, the document now features better formatting and more specific instructions.

entries.title: Overview of Google Play Instant
entries.summary: The documentation for Google Play Instant has been updated. The primary changes include a critical announcement that starting in December 2025, Instant Apps will no longer be available, and developers should use deeplinks to redirect users to their regular app or game. Additionally, the formatting has been updated with more informative notes and cautions.

entries.title: SDK release notes
entries.summary: This document has been updated to announce that Google Play Instant will be discontinued starting December 2025. Developers are encouraged to use deep links to direct users to their regular apps instead.

entries.title: Code samples
entries.summary: This document provides information about Google Play Instant samples. The core change is the deprecation of Google Play Instant, with Instant Apps ceasing to be available after December 2025. Developers are encouraged to use deep links to direct users to their regular apps or games. Additionally, the formatting of the note about the samples has been updated.

entries.title: Google Play Instant: Technical requirements checklist
entries.summary: The document has been updated to reflect that Google Play Instant will no longer be available after December 2025. This change emphasizes the need for developers to direct users to their regular app or game using deeplinks for continued user engagement. The document also includes formatting changes, and some slight wording changes for clarity.

entries.title: App Startup
entries.summary: This document introduces the App Startup library, explaining its use for initializing components at application startup to improve performance.  The changes include minor wording improvements, better formatting, and clarification on how to disable automatic initialization and manually call component initializers.

entries.title: Add components to your project
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the JCenter repository being read-only. The warning about JCenter was updated to use a more prominent callout block.

entries.title: Additional Resources for Architecture Components
entries.summary: The documentation for additional architecture component resources has been updated. Formatting changes include adding blank lines before and after list items in codelabs, training, blog posts, and videos, and splitting lines in blog posts and videos to make the content more readable.

entries.title: Integrate Lifecycle with Compose
entries.summary: This document introduces APIs in the Lifecycle library for integrating with Jetpack Compose. It explains how to collect lifecycle state using flows and run code based on lifecycle events using LifecycleEffects, LifecycleStartEffect, and LifecycleResumeEffect, providing examples of their usage and clarifying their purpose in managing lifecycles within Compose applications. The document was updated to reflect the version 2.7.0 of the Lifecycle library.

entries.title: Use Kotlin coroutines with lifecycle-aware components
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of using Kotlin coroutines with lifecycle-aware components, including ViewModelScope, LifecycleScope, and LiveData. The changes include improved formatting, more descriptive explanations, and clarifications on using repeatOnLifecycle and flowWithLifecycle for lifecycle-aware flow collection. It also adds a note regarding the use of when methods with lifecycle and a warning on the preferability of repeatOnLifecycle over launchWhenX APIs.

entries.title: Handling lifecycles with lifecycle-aware components
entries.summary: This document introduces the concept of lifecycle-aware components in Android development. It explains how to use the `androidx.lifecycle` package to create components that automatically adjust their behavior based on the lifecycle state of activities and fragments, promoting better code organization and maintainability.

entries.title: LiveData overview
entries.summary: The documentation for LiveData has been updated. The changes include improvements in formatting, the addition of the NOTE blocks, and minor rewording for clarity. The content remains largely the same, focusing on the core concepts and advantages of using LiveData.

entries.title: Paging 2 library overview
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to use the `[!CAUTION]` and `[!NOTE]` callout blocks for important information, improving readability and highlighting key points about the deprecated Paging library version. This change makes it easier for developers to quickly identify warnings and notes within the documentation.

entries.title: Gather paged data
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the deprecation of the Paging library and directs users to the Paging 3 guides. It also incorporates formatting changes, including the use of callout boxes and minor adjustments to existing content for improved clarity and readability.

entries.title: Manage and present loading states
entries.summary: This document describes how to manage and present loading states using the Paging library.  It explains how the library exposes loading states through the `LoadState` class and how to use listeners or adapters to display loading indicators in the UI. The changes include formatting updates, and clarifying language, and improved code samples.

entries.title: Test your Paging implementation
entries.summary: This document provides an updated guide on testing Paging library implementations. Key changes include enhanced explanations of UI layer tests with `asSnapshot()`, testing transformations with `asPagingSourceFactory()`, and data layer tests for `PagingSource` and `RemoteMediator`, along with improved code examples and clarifications.

entries.title: Display paged lists
entries.summary: The documentation updates the UI section of the Paging Library guide. It includes formatting improvements, clarifies the use of deprecated versions, and updates links. Additionally, it improves the formatting of code snippets and adds more detailed explanations about placeholders.

entries.title: Migrate to Paging 3
entries.summary: This document details the migration from Paging 2 to Paging 3, highlighting the key changes in components like DataSource, PagedList, and PagedListAdapter, as well as the benefits of adopting Paging 3.  It explains how to migrate each component, and mentions backwards compatibility allowing for a phased migration.  It also points to where to find additional information like defining a data source or the RecyclerView adapter.

entries.title: Page from network and database
entries.summary: This document introduces how to use Paging 3 library with a local database to improve user experience when network connections are unreliable. It explains how to coordinate data loads using RemoteMediator, the Paging lifecycle, and provides implementation examples using Room.

entries.title: Paging library overview
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to use the standard Markdown callout syntax, replacing the bolded 'Note:' with a more structured '[!NOTE]' callout block. This change enhances readability and consistency with other documentation.

entries.title: Load and display paged data
entries.summary: This document provides a comprehensive guide on using the Paging library v3 to load and display paged data in an Android application. It covers defining data sources using `PagingSource`, setting up data streams with `Pager`, and implementing a `RecyclerView` adapter with `PagingDataAdapter`.

entries.title: Transform data streams
entries.summary: This document describes how to transform data streams when using the Paging library. It explains how to apply basic transformations like converting data types, filtering data, and adding list separators, along with code examples in Kotlin and Java. It emphasizes keeping repository and UI constructs separate and avoiding duplicate work by using the `cachedIn()` operation.

entries.title: Architecture Component Release Notes
entries.summary: This document provides release notes for Architecture Components. The changes involve formatting adjustments to improve readability, particularly by reformatting links to the AndroidX release notes and the release note archive. These modifications enhance the presentation of the documentation without changing the information provided.

entries.title: Save UI states
entries.summary: The documentation updates several "Key Point", "Note", and "Warning" sections by changing their formatting, specifically by adding a `> [!IMPORTANT]`, `> [!NOTE]`, or `> [!WARNING]` block to them. These formatting changes are meant to emphasize the importance of the corresponding information.

entries.title: ViewModel overview
entries.summary: The documentation for ViewModel has been updated with minor formatting changes, added markdown callouts (NOTE, IMPORTANT, CAUTION), and improved clarity in several sections. These changes refine the explanation of ViewModel's benefits, including persistence through configuration changes and access to business logic. The guide also clarifies ViewModel usage within Jetpack Compose and provides implementation examples.

entries.title: ViewModel Scoping APIs
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to replace the markdown formatting of the notes with the standard "NOTE" block formatting for improved readability and consistency.

entries.title: ViewModel APIs cheat sheet
entries.summary: The ViewModel cheat sheet documentation was updated to include a description of the cheat sheet's content, and added a link to Android Jetpack. This makes the purpose of the documentation and its relationship to the broader Android ecosystem more explicit.

entries.title: Create ViewModels with dependencies
entries.summary: The documentation for ViewModel factories has been updated to improve clarity and formatting. Changes include the addition of Markdown note and warning blocks for better visual emphasis, and minor adjustments to code examples. The table formatting has also been improved for readability.

entries.title: Saved State module for ViewModel
entries.summary: The documentation for the Saved State module for ViewModel has been updated with improved formatting using admonitions for Key Points, Important notes, and Cautions.  The documentation now clarifies the use cases for `SavedStateHandle`, explaining its role in handling transient UI state and how to integrate with observable data holders like LiveData and StateFlow.  Also clarified the behavior of `SavedStateHandle` including when data is saved and potential data loss scenarios when using LiveData.

entries.title: Data Binding Library
entries.summary: This document introduces the Data Binding Library, explaining how it enables declarative UI binding to data sources.  It highlights the advantages of using the library, such as simplifying activities, improving performance, and preventing memory leaks, and recommends using View Binding in some cases. It also updates links and formatting of the existing documentation.

entries.title: Bind layout views to Architecture Components
entries.summary: The change updates the `android:onCheckedChanged` attribute in the data binding example. The original code had a syntax error in the lambda expression, and it has been corrected. This matters because it fixes a potential error in the data binding, ensuring the `rememberMeChanged()` method is correctly called when the checkbox state changes.

entries.title: Binding adapters
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to replace some "Note" sections with "NOTE" blocks for improved formatting. Additionally, a note was added to demonstrate how to access values in a map using the `object.key` notation.

entries.title: Layouts and binding expressions
entries.summary: This document introduces data binding expressions, explaining how they work, the syntax used, and how to use them with data objects and resources. It also highlights the features and limitations of the expression language.

entries.title: Generated binding classes
entries.summary: This document explains how the Data Binding Library generates binding classes that provide access to the layout's variables and views. The updated content includes formatting changes, improvements to clarity, and minor code example adjustments.

entries.title: Work with observable data objects
entries.summary: This document introduces observability in the context of data binding, explaining how to make objects, fields, and collections observable so that changes in data automatically update the UI. It provides examples for each type of observable class, including observable fields, collections, and objects, and discusses how to use lifecycle-aware objects like StateFlow and LiveData.

entries.title: Get started
entries.summary: The documentation for the Data Binding Library has been updated with minor formatting and wording changes. The primary changes involve enhanced clarity and consistency in formatting, including the use of markdown for emphasis and improved phrasing for better understanding of the data binding setup and usage. These updates do not introduce new functionality but refine the presentation of existing content.

entries.title: Two-way data binding
entries.summary: This document introduces two-way data binding in Android. It explains how to use the `@={}` notation to bind data and listen for user updates simultaneously, and how to implement it with custom attributes using `@InverseBindingAdapter` and `@InverseBindingMethod` annotations. The document also covers the use of converters and how to avoid infinite loops when implementing two-way data binding.

entries.title: View binding
entries.summary: This document introduces View Binding, a feature in Android Jetpack that simplifies writing code interacting with views. The changes include updates to the title, URLs, and formatting, with specific examples on using View Binding within Activities and Fragments, code snippets, and differences with findViewByID and data binding.

entries.title: Migrate from Kotlin synthetics to Jetpack view binding
entries.summary: This document guides developers on migrating from Kotlin synthetics for view binding to Jetpack view binding, as Kotlin Android Extensions are deprecated. The changes involve updating the Gradle file and activity/fragment classes to utilize the generated binding classes instead of synthetic properties.

entries.title: Guide to Android app modularization
entries.summary: The documentation for Android app modularization has been updated to clarify the definition of a multi-module project and to improve the wording for better clarity. Formatting changes include converting HTML elements to Markdown and restructuring for better readability.

entries.title: Common modularization patterns
entries.summary: This document introduces modularization patterns for Android apps, emphasizing the principles of high cohesion and low coupling. It describes different module types, including data, feature, app, common, and test modules, along with their responsibilities and use cases. The update includes formatting changes, the addition of a video link, and the introduction of new notes and tips to clarify concepts.

entries.title: Diagnose and fix ANRs
entries.summary: This document provides more context and improves clarity regarding ANRs, including the types of ANRs, how to diagnose and fix them. There are also additional code snippets, flowcharts, and diagrams.

entries.title: Find the unresponsive thread
entries.summary: This document, which details how to identify the unresponsive thread in an Application Not Responding (ANR) stack dump, has been updated. The changes include formatting updates, such as converting inline URLs to markdown links and minor text adjustments. Additionally, the document now explicitly mentions the Perfetto traces as the best way to investigate the reasons behind slow binder calls.

entries.title: Keep your app responsive
entries.summary: The document was updated to provide clearer and more detailed guidance on avoiding Application Not Responding (ANR) errors. It includes clarifications on thread management, startup performance, BroadcastReceiver best practices, and the use of performance tools.

entries.title: App hibernation
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to replace markdown-style notes with the new callout syntax. This change affects several sections, including usage considerations and exemption requests, improving the visual clarity of important information.

entries.title: Adopt optimizations incrementally
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on adopting R8 optimizations incrementally to improve app performance and size. It explains how to limit optimizations initially, use compat mode, and scope optimizations to specific parts of the app to avoid issues during the transition. It offers practical advice on using tree shaking, disabling obfuscation, and employing package-wide keep rules, while emphasizing the importance of gradually removing these temporary workarounds for optimal optimization.

entries.title: Customize which resources to keep
entries.summary: This document introduces improvements in formatting and wording, with enhanced clarity. It also adds better explanations for using `resConfigs` and how the build process handles resource merging. Minor corrections and formatting changes have also been implemented.

entries.title: Enable app optimization with R8
entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) version 9.1. It highlights a new default behavior where R8 repackages classes to further compact DEX files, eliminating the need for a specific option. This change affects how Android apps are optimized for size and performance, and the document directs users to relevant resources for further details and opt-out instructions.

entries.title: Use R8 in full mode
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to replace markdown notes with the more modern callout blocks. The examples in the document did not change.

entries.title: Add global options
entries.summary: The documentation for global options has been updated to reflect changes in Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) versions. Specifically, it clarifies that `-repackageclasses` is the default setting for apps since AGP 9.1 and `-allowaccessmodification` is enabled by default if full optimization with R8 is enabled from AGP 8.2. It also adds an option to opt out of the `-repackageclasses` setting.

entries.title: Target a build variant
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify how to add custom keep rules for different build variants, improving readability with added formatting and a note callout, improving clarity and maintainability.

entries.title: Test the optimization
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to fix formatting issues and minor wording improvements. It contains slight adjustments to the formatting of the text and URLs, enhancing readability without introducing any functional changes.

entries.title: Get your App Performance Score
entries.summary: This document introduces the App Performance Score, a tool for evaluating and improving Android app performance.  It outlines how the score is calculated using dynamic and static assessments and provides guidance on interpreting the results and implementing recommendations to optimize app startup and rendering performance.

entries.title: App startup analysis and optimization
entries.summary: This document provides more details on app startup analysis and optimization. The document has been updated to include more details on using Macrobenchmark library, more explanation on the Time To Full Display (TTFD) metric and improve the overall readability of the document.

entries.title: Best practices for app optimization
entries.summary: This document provides best practices for optimizing Android app startup performance. The changes primarily refine the text for better readability and formatting, updating links, and clarifying the explanations of techniques like Baseline Profiles, startup profiles, App Startup library, lazy loading, ViewStubs, splash screens, scalable image types, performance APIs and cold startup traces. These changes aim to help developers improve app startup time and responsiveness, resulting in a better user experience.

entries.title: Case study: how the Gmail Wear OS team improved their app startup by 50%
entries.summary: This document provides a case study on how the Gmail Wear OS team optimized app startup performance. The changes include rephrasing for clarity, adding and modifying notes and tips, and minor adjustments to improve readability. The goal is to guide developers in analyzing and improving their app's startup time by leveraging tools like Perfetto and addressing bottlenecks such as CPU contention and binder transactions.

entries.title: Set up your environment for performance testing
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on setting up an environment for Android app performance testing. The changes primarily involve reformatting text for improved readability and adding some minor clarifications about using release builds and custom traces. There are no major changes to the content.

entries.title: How the Android Calendar team improved app startup and jank with Baseline Profiles
entries.summary: This document describes the Android Calendar team's use of Baseline Profiles to improve app startup and reduce jank. The changes include improvements to the clarity and formatting of the documentation, including updated code examples and more specific details on implementation and impact.

entries.title: Confirm Startup Profiles optimization
entries.summary: This document describes how to confirm that Startup Profiles are working correctly. The changes involve minor text adjustments for clarity, formatting improvements, and updated instructions for verifying DEX layout optimization using Android Studio and bundle metadata. These updates improve the user experience by providing more straightforward guidance for validating the optimization process.

entries.title: Create Baseline Profiles for a library
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to improve readability and clarity.  The changes include minor formatting adjustments, such as improved spacing and line breaks, and minor rewording for clarity.  These changes do not significantly alter the content's technical aspects, focusing instead on improving the presentation of the information for the user.

entries.title: Debug Baseline Profiles
entries.summary: The documentation for debugging Baseline Profiles was updated to improve clarity and provide more concise instructions. It includes changes such as clarifying when to use custom run configurations, and providing a note about the necessity of custom configurations when using the Baseline Profile module wizard. Additionally, notes were added, and text was updated for consistency. These updates help developers more easily set up and verify Baseline Profiles, improving app performance through optimized code execution.

entries.title: Manually create and measure Baseline Profiles
entries.summary: This document underwent several revisions including formatting changes, and some rephrasing for clarity.  Key updates include note formatting changes, and minor clarifications within the guide. There were also code examples updated from 'scdoc' to backticks.

entries.title: Benchmark in Continuous Integration
entries.summary: This document provides information about running benchmarks in Continuous Integration (CI) to track performance. The changes include improvements in formatting and clarity, such as using code blocks and notes, and the addition of details for specifying the output directory for benchmark results.

entries.title: Control your app from Macrobenchmark
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to improve readability and clarity. Changes include text refinements, code block formatting, and a note converted to a more emphasized format. These adjustments enhance the user experience by making the content easier to understand.

entries.title: Macrobenchmark instrumentation arguments
entries.summary: This document describes the instrumentation arguments available for configuring the behavior of the Macrobenchmark library. The changes include improvements in clarity, formatting and the addition of new arguments such as  `androidx.benchmark.killExistingPerfettoRecordings` and updates to existing argument descriptions for better understanding, and the correction of typo.

entries.title: Microbenchmark instrumentation arguments
entries.summary: This document describes the instrumentation arguments available for configuring Microbenchmark behavior. The changes include improvements in formatting, and updates to the descriptions of existing arguments like `androidx.benchmark.cpuEventCounter.enable`, `androidx.benchmark.dryRunMode.enable`, `androidx.benchmark.killExistingPerfettoRecordings`, `androidx.benchmark.output.enable`, `androidx.benchmark.profiling.mode`, `androidx.benchmark.suppressErrors`, `additionalTestOutputDir`, and `listener`.  A new argument, `androidx.benchmark.iterations`, has been added, and the descriptions for existing arguments have been revised for clarity and accuracy. These changes provide more control and flexibility when running benchmarks.

entries.title: Microbenchmark
entries.summary: The documentation introduces the Jetpack Microbenchmark library, which enables benchmarking of Android native code (Kotlin or Java) directly from Android Studio. This allows developers to measure code performance and allocation counts, providing valuable insights for optimization.

entries.title: Build Microbenchmarks without Gradle
entries.summary: This document describes how to configure microbenchmarks for non-Gradle build systems. The changes clarify how to set the debuggable flag correctly and provide recommendations for compilation, minification, optimization, and code coverage to ensure accurate benchmark results.

entries.title: Inspect performance
entries.summary: The documentation was updated with minor formatting changes and link updates for clarity. Also, the document was updated to include a video link.

entries.title: JankStats Library
entries.summary: This document provides examples and instructions for using the JankStats library, including initialization, reporting, and adding state. It has been updated to include a note clarifying an `IllegalStateException` condition and uses markdown formatting for the note.

entries.title: Memory allocation among processes
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of Android's memory management, detailing how memory is allocated among system processes and user applications, and how the OS responds to low-memory situations. The changes include improved clarity, formatting, and added details on memory page types, the kernel swap daemon, and the low-memory killer.

entries.title: Monitor performance
entries.summary: The documentation introduces a video and reformats text for better readability by adding line breaks and fixing formatting inconsistencies. These changes enhance the presentation of the content for easier comprehension and improve the overall user experience.

entries.title: Performance class
entries.summary: The documentation for Performance Class has been updated to clarify device capabilities, and how developers can utilize them at runtime. Also, the document now points out the usage of the Jetpack Core Performance library for retrieving the device's Media Performance Class (MPC) level.

entries.title: Examples of performance measurement and analysis
entries.summary: This document provides examples of how to measure and improve Android app performance using systrace with Macrobenchmark and the Android Studio memory profiler. The changes include improved formatting, clearer descriptions, and additional details on analyzing performance bottlenecks such as monitor contention, synchronous binder transactions, and I/O operations.

entries.title: Analyze power use with Battery Historian
entries.summary: This document introduces Battery Historian, a tool for analyzing battery consumption on Android devices. It describes how to interpret system-wide and app-specific data within the tool to identify and address power-draining behaviors. The document also highlights key areas like the system-wide view, app-specific data, and provides command-line instructions to enhance data collection.

entries.title: Optimize server-initiated network use
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify and rephrase existing content, enhancing readability. Additionally, a new callout block was introduced to emphasize best practices for FCM message priority, advising the use of normal priority whenever possible to optimize device power consumption.

entries.title: Analyze network traffic data with the Network Traffic tool
entries.summary: The document has been updated to reflect the deprecation of the Network Traffic tool, replacing references with the Network Profiler. Hyperlinks have been updated to reflect developer.android.com domain, and some formatting changes have been introduced, including an addition of a caution block and updates to the training guide references.

entries.title: Power management resource limits
entries.summary: This document provides detailed information on Android's power management resource limits, explaining how the system prioritizes apps' requests based on device state, app state, and standby buckets. The document clarifies how resource limits are enforced, including deferring execution and reducing frequency/duration of app activities, and outlines the impact of device and app states on these limits.

entries.title: Profile battery usage with Batterystats and Battery Historian
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect that Battery Historian is no longer actively maintained and suggests alternative tools like system tracing, Macrobenchmark power metric, or the Power Profiler. The document also includes updated instructions and formatting for installing and using Battery Historian.

entries.title: Inspect GPU rendering speed and overdraw
entries.summary: The document was updated to improve clarity and formatting. Changes include enhanced descriptions of the Profile GPU Rendering tool, descriptions of the output, and updated table formatting to improve readability.

entries.title: Reduce overdraw
entries.summary: The documentation for overdraw has been updated with more information on what overdraw is, how to identify it, and how to fix it. The changes clarify the definition of overdraw, explain the painter's algorithm, and provide more details on using debugging tools like Debug GPU Overdraw and Profile GPU rendering. The document also includes more detailed explanations and best practices for reducing overdraw, such as removing unnecessary backgrounds, flattening view hierarchies, and reducing transparency. These updates help developers optimize their apps for better performance.

entries.title: Capture a system trace on the command line
entries.summary: This document provides updated instructions for using the `systrace` command-line tool to generate system trace reports. It includes more context about alternative tracing methods like the Android Studio Profilers and the System Tracing app, and clarifies the necessary setup steps for running `systrace`.

entries.title: Convenience tips
entries.summary: This document describes the native tracing API, `trace.h`, used for performance analysis in Android apps. Key changes include clarifying memory limitations on older API levels, and minor text refinements for clarity and formatting throughout the document.

entries.title: Define custom events
entries.summary: The documentation for custom events in system tracing has been updated to clarify the purpose of custom tracing and how to use it with Macrobenchmark. It also emphasizes the importance of using the Kotlin extension function for ease of use and error prevention.

entries.title: Navigate a Systrace report
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with some minor formatting changes, added a note and improved the layout of the keyboard shortcuts table to enhance readability. These changes improve clarity and user experience while navigating the Systrace report.

entries.title: Profile types and when each is helpful
entries.summary: The documentation for profile types overview was updated to replace the markdown syntax for a note. The change replaces the markdown syntax for a note to the 'Note' with a block with the syntax '> [!NOTE]' to improve its rendering within the documentation.

entries.title: Debug commands for local profiling
entries.summary: This document describes how to use `adb` commands to debug the `ProfilingManager` locally. It details how to disable rate limiting and retain unredacted traces, both of which are useful for local profiling and investigation. This is important because it allows developers to gather more detailed profile data and troubleshoot issues related to profiling more effectively.

entries.title: Overview
entries.summary: The documentation introduces the `ProfilingManager` API, which allows developers to programmatically collect performance profiles, such as system traces. The changes include a clearer introductory sentence, formatting updates, and the addition of an `IMPORTANT` note. These changes clarify the API's purpose and provide developers with a concise understanding of its application for collecting end-user performance profiles.

entries.title: Retrieve and analyze profiling data
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to use the standard markdown callout blocks (`> [!NOTE]`, `> [!IMPORTANT]`) for notes and key points, improving readability and consistency. This change makes the information more prominent and easier to understand.

entries.title: Profiling limitations
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to clarify how rate limiting works in the ProfilingManager. It provides more detail on the cost assigned to profiles, the time periods used for rate limiting (hourly, daily, weekly), and the errors that can occur when limits are reached. Additionally, the document now emphasizes that the system-level rate limiter is a global limit shared by all apps.

entries.title: Android vitals
entries.summary: This document provides minor updates to improve clarity and formatting. Changes include clarifying that Android vitals uses user-perceived metrics, updating links, and minor text enhancements.

entries.title: Excessive Mobile Network Usage in Background
entries.summary: The documentation on background network usage was updated to improve clarity and formatting. Changes include rewording sentences, adding links, and updating the warning about Battery Historian. These changes aim to provide more accurate and accessible information for developers to optimize their app's network usage and battery performance.

entries.title: Excessive Wi-Fi Scanning in the Background
entries.summary: The documentation on background Wi-Fi scanning has been updated for clarity and consistency.  It clarifies when an app is considered to be running in the background and provides more specific links and formatting improvements.  These changes enhance readability and provide more accurate information for developers.

entries.title: Crashes
entries.summary: The documentation for Android app crashes has been updated to include minor formatting changes and improve clarity.  The note regarding Wear OS apps has been changed to use the correct formatting.

entries.title: Excessive battery usage
entries.summary: The documentation for excessive battery usage in Wear OS watch faces has been updated with minor formatting changes, emphasis on key information using callout boxes, and clarifications. These updates aim to provide clearer guidance on optimizing watch face apps for battery efficiency and reducing visibility penalties in Google Play.

entries.title: Excessive partial wake locks
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to clarify how Android Vitals identifies excessive partial wake lock usage, including the threshold (2 or more hours in a 24-hour period) and the exemptions applied. Additional details on the Android Vitals dashboard are provided, along with the impact on app visibility on Google Play.

entries.title: App startup time
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to provide a more comprehensive explanation of app startup, including why fast startup is important for user experience and retention. It also includes an expanded explanation of the different app startup states (cold, warm, and hot) and how to identify and measure startup time using Perfetto. The introductory text was rewritten, and the document includes additional details about startup time metrics (TTID, TTFD) and provides more context on the processes involved in app launch.

entries.title: Low memory killers
entries.summary: This document has been updated to include better formatting, improve clarity, and adds more information for the Unreal Engine's Memory Insights. These changes enhance the readability and comprehensiveness of the documentation.

entries.title: Permission Denials
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on how to handle Android app permissions, focusing on user privacy and engagement. It explains how Android Vitals helps gauge user behavior by tracking permission denials and offers best practices to minimize them, such as requesting only necessary permissions and explaining them in context.

entries.title: Slow rendering
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with minor formatting changes, added a "Note" block, and improved clarity in several sections. Specifically, the introduction was reworked, and the Systrace section was enhanced with updated links and more information about alerts and custom trace events.

entries.title: Slow Sessions (games only)
entries.summary: This document introduces the "Slow Sessions" metric in Android Vitals, a new metric in the Google Play console used to measure frame-rate performance of games. It explains what constitutes a slow session, how to measure frame rates using `dumpsys surfaceflinger timestats`, and provides solutions using Android Frame Pacing (Swappy), Vulkan, and ADPF.

entries.title: Stuck partial wake locks
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to improve readability and clarity. Changes include better formatting, more descriptive language, and improved organization. This makes the documentation easier to understand and follow, which helps developers to diagnose and fix stuck wake lock issues in their Android applications more effectively.

entries.title: Excessive wakeups
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated with minor formatting changes, including improved readability and clarity through the use of line breaks and enhanced formatting of code snippets and API references. It also contains slight improvements in wording for better comprehension.

entries.title: Create App Links for Instant Apps
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Instant App Links has been updated. The changes clarify the requirements for app links in instant apps, emphasizing the need for both HTTP and HTTPS support in intent filters. It also reiterates the rules regarding domain association and provides more detailed reminders for creating app links, including assetlinks.json requirements and best practices for host attributes.

entries.title: Limit loading in on-device Android containers
entries.summary: The documentation clarifies the definition of "On-device Android containers" and adds a sentence explaining how to prevent these containers from loading or proxying a Play Store app. It also improves formatting.

entries.title: Get a result from an activity
entries.summary: This document provides more formatting changes, adds note blocks, and clarifies the use of `registerForActivityResult()` and `ActivityResultRegistry` with lifecycle management. These changes improve readability and provide better guidance for developers using the Activity Result APIs.

entries.title: Sending the user to another app
entries.summary: The documentation for sending intents was updated to include a video, fix broken links, and improve clarity. It also updated text for better formatting and more accurate information.

entries.title: Android for Cars overview
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Auto has been updated to reflect the use of the > [!NOTE] admonition for notes instead of the markdown bold format. Also the video application description was updated to remove a space between the asterisks.

entries.title: Add support for Android Auto to your templated app
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify the location of the `automotive_app_desc.xml` file within the project directory and to slightly reword a sentence for clarity. This is important for developers to correctly set up their Android Auto applications.

entries.title: Add support for Android Automotive OS to your templated app
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on building Android Automotive OS apps using the Car App Library. It covers the necessary steps, including creating an automotive module, declaring support in the manifest, updating Gradle dependencies, and declaring CarAppService and CarAppActivity.

entries.title: Build an internet of things app
entries.summary: The documentation for IOT apps has been updated to improve readability and clarity. The changes include reformatting text for better flow and adding links to relevant resources, ensuring developers have the necessary information to build IOT car applications. No new functionality was introduced.

entries.title: Use the Android for Cars App Library
entries.summary: The documentation for the Car App Library was updated to include an IMPORTANT block, replacing the previous markdown formatting with a callout block. This change emphasizes Google's strong stance on driver distraction and the requirements for apps to be listed on Google Play for Android Auto and Android Automotive OS, clarifying the importance of adhering to these guidelines for app developers.

entries.title: Car Hardware APIs
entries.summary: The documentation for the Car Hardware API was updated to improve clarity and formatting. Specifically, example code snippets were added for checking vehicle heading, and a caution regarding CarSensors data on Android Automotive OS was enhanced with a more prominent warning style and formatting. These changes aim to assist developers in utilizing car hardware features, particularly on Android Automotive OS, by providing clearer usage examples and highlighting important platform-specific limitations.

entries.title: Record from the car microphone
entries.summary: The documentation updates include minor wording adjustments and formatting changes in the "car-microphone.md" file. There are no substantial modifications impacting functionality or introducing new features. Therefore, the changes are considered trivial.

entries.title: Create your CarAppService and Session
entries.summary: This document describes the implementation of `CarAppService` and its `onCreateSession` method. The changes include minor wording adjustments and formatting updates to improve readability, but do not introduce new functionality.

entries.title: Connection API
entries.summary: The documentation updates the warning regarding CarConnection API and Android 14 compatibility. It clarifies that apps targeting Android 14 and higher must use Car App Library version 1.3.0-beta01 or later to avoid exceptions related to receiver export behavior. Apps targeting Android 13 or lower are not affected by this change.

entries.title: Constraints API
entries.summary: The documentation for the constraints-api.md file was updated to use the Android Markdown syntax for an important alert. This change highlights that the host sets the content limits, and these limits cannot be modified by client apps. The link to the integers.xml resource file remains the same.

entries.title: Draw maps
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify how to handle theme changes. It also added a new admonition. This matters because it provides developers with better guidelines to render their app based on the current system theme.

entries.title: Add inline CarIcons for rows
entries.summary: The documentation for inline car icons has been updated to use a more modern callout block for the note regarding API level compatibility. This improves readability and consistency with the current documentation style.

entries.title: Install the Car App library
entries.summary: The documentation updated the emphasis on the use of alpha and beta releases of the Jetpack library for car apps. It now uses a callout box to highlight the importance of exercising caution when using newly introduced or experimental APIs in those releases, as they may lead to compatibility issues.

entries.title: Request permissions
entries.summary: The documentation for requesting permissions in Android Auto apps was updated to use the markdown warning block. This change replaces the inline warning with a markdown warning block, improving readability and highlighting the dependency on `androidx.car.app:1.7.0-alpha01` or later for correct permission dialog behavior on Android 14+ devices. This is important to ensure that apps correctly prompt the user for permissions when run on compatible Android Auto devices.

entries.title: Implement screen navigation
entries.summary: The documentation for screen navigation in Android Automotive has been updated. The changes include a minor wording adjustment to clarify the behavior of back actions in message templates and how new screens are pushed.

entries.title: Set up your project
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to replace the markdown syntax for bolded text and notes with the `> [!IMPORTANT]`, `> [!CAUTION]`, and `> [!NOTE]` syntax for callout boxes. Also, the `<var translate="no">` tags were removed.

entries.title: Create your start screen
entries.summary: The documentation for the start-screen.md file was updated to remove a trailing whitespace.

entries.title: Template restrictions
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to use the 'CAUTION' and 'NOTE' callout blocks instead of bold text for emphasis. This change improves the visual clarity and consistency of the documentation.

entries.title: Add text string variants
entries.summary: The documentation provides examples and instructions on how to use text string variants with CarText in Android Automotive apps. The changes include minor formatting adjustments and link updates.

entries.title: Build a templated media app
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the use of Markdown note and caution blocks instead of bolded text for emphasis. The manifest and API level requirements, along with the code example, remained unchanged.

entries.title: Build a navigation app
entries.summary: The document was updated with minor changes to the manifest file examples for Android Automotive OS navigation apps. These changes primarily involve fixing potential typos and formatting inconsistencies within the XML code, ensuring proper declaration of services, intent filters, and permissions. These updates are essential for maintaining the accuracy and usability of the code examples for developers building navigation apps.

entries.title: Build a point of interest app
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on developing Point of Interest (POI) apps for Android Auto. It covers how to declare category support, access map templates, refresh content, and integrate with Google Assistant using App Actions.

entries.title: Build a point of interest app
entries.summary: The document was updated to use the `androidx.car.app.category.POI` category instead of the deprecated `androidx.car.app.category.PARKING` and `androidx.car.app.category.CHARGING` categories. Also, the documentation was updated to clarify how to refresh the PlaceListMapTemplate content, and some minor formatting and wording changes.

entries.title: Build a point of interest app
entries.summary: The changes ensure that developers are using the correct and current categories for their POI apps, aligning with the latest Car App Library versions. The refresh content update helps developers implement content refresh functionality in their apps.

entries.title: Build a weather app
entries.summary: The documentation for weather apps has been updated to clarify the purpose of weather applications in cars, emphasizing their ability to provide weather information for current locations or along a route, and offering navigation capabilities.  The document also updates formatting, link structures and emphasis.

entries.title: Car ready mobile apps
entries.summary: This document outlines the Car ready mobile apps program, which allows eligible mobile apps in the video, games, and browser categories to be distributed to cars with Android Automotive OS. It details eligibility requirements, testing procedures, and how to participate and opt-out of the program. The document has been updated to clarify the launch date, the availability of apps, and the impact of the program on app distribution.

entries.title: Build communication apps for Android Auto
entries.summary: The document has been updated to use a more descriptive introductory paragraph and a more modern callout block for the tip. This change provides a better overview of communication features on Android Auto, improving readability and user experience.

entries.title: Build calling experiences for Android Auto
entries.summary: The documentation for calling apps has been updated to use the `> [!IMPORTANT]` syntax for important notes. This change improves the visual emphasis of the critical information for developers, making it more noticeable. Additionally, it clarifies the requirement to include both calling and messaging categories in the same intent filter if an app supports both features.

entries.title: Extend messaging notifications to Android Auto
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Auto messaging notifications has been updated. The changes include adding a description, and updating the formatting of "Important", "Warning", and "Tip" sections using the `> [!IMPORTANT]`, `> [!WARNING]`, and `> [!TIP]` syntax. These updates improve readability and clarify important information for developers integrating messaging features with Android Auto. The documentation also clarifies the use of `PendingIntent.FLAG_MUTABLE`.

entries.title: Build templated messaging experiences for Android Auto
entries.summary: The documentation for templated messaging has been updated to use the 'Note' and 'Tip' syntax using the markdown syntax. This change improves the visual clarity and formatting of the document for developers, making important information stand out more effectively.

entries.title: Distribute to cars
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of how to distribute Android apps for Android Auto and Android Automotive OS (AAOS). It outlines guidelines and requirements for different app categories to help developers successfully launch their apps on these platforms.

entries.title: Media apps for cars overview
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect formatting changes, improved code examples, and more helpful content regarding the development of media apps for Android Auto and AAOS. Formatting and some wording were updated in several sections, including the section about creating audio media apps. The documentation has also added or updated some CAUTION and NOTE blocks.

entries.title: Add support for Android Auto to your media app
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Auto media support has been updated. The changes include a minor formatting adjustment to emphasize the key information regarding existing media browser service implementation by using a callout. This change aims to improve clarity and highlight the prerequisites for integrating media apps with Android Auto.

entries.title: Add Android Automotive OS support to your media app
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Automotive OS media apps was updated. Changes include rewording, improved formatting with admonitions, and clarifying the steps for project setup, particularly manifest configuration and Gradle dependencies.

entries.title: Configure manifest files
entries.summary: This document describes how to configure your app's manifest files to integrate with Android Auto and Android Automotive OS (AAOS), focusing on declaring the media browser service and specifying app icons, labels, and accent colors.  Changes include minor formatting adjustments, clarifying descriptions, and updating code snippets for better readability.

entries.title: Media browser service overview
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of creating a media browser service for Android Auto and Android Automotive OS (AAOS). The primary change involves clarifying the classes to extend (MediaBrowserServiceCompat or MediaLibraryService) and how Android Auto/AAOS utilizes the service for content browsing and media playback control. This matters because it provides essential context for developers building media applications for car platforms, ensuring compatibility and proper functionality within the Android Auto/AAOS environment.

entries.title: Display browsable search results
entries.summary: This document describes how to enhance the search experience in Android Auto and Android Automotive OS by enabling searches without voice and displaying search results. The changes include clarifying the purpose of the page, refining the descriptions of how to support search, and updating formatting.

entries.title: Build your content hierarchy
entries.summary: This document introduces updates to the content hierarchy implementation for Android Auto and Android Automotive OS (AAOS) media browser services. The changes include clarification and better formatting of code snippets, and updated package validation guidance including allowing Google Assistant access to the MediaBrowserService. It also highlights the importance of root menu structure, tab limits, and the use of root hints to optimize content display.

entries.title: Apply content styles
entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to content styles in Android Automotive OS (AAOS) media applications. It clarifies how to apply content styles for browsable and playable items, including setting defaults using BrowserRoot extras and overriding styles for specific items or their descendants. The updates also provide clearer guidance on grouping media items using title hints, emphasizing the importance of contiguous blocks for correct grouping and explaining how the order of items impacts the grouping logic.

entries.title: Implement custom browse actions
entries.summary: This document describes how to implement custom browse actions in an Android MediaBrowserServiceCompat for Android Auto. It explains how to define actions, attach them to MediaItems, handle user interactions, and update action states. The document has been updated with minor improvements to clarify the implementation steps, and formatting.

entries.title: Media browser service lifecycle

entries.title: Implement distraction safeguards
entries.summary: This document explains how to implement distraction safeguards in Android Auto media apps. It focuses on preventing automatic audio playback, such as alarms, and managing how advertisements are displayed to minimize driver distraction. The document provides instructions on using the `CarConnection` API and setting the `METADATA_KEY_IS_ADVERTISEMENT` key.

entries.title: Enable playback control
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to use the `> [!NOTE]` syntax for callout boxes instead of the previous `| **Note:**` format for note sections. This change improves the visual consistency and clarity of the documentation.

entries.title: Handle errors
entries.summary: The documentation for handling errors in Android Auto and Android Automotive has been updated to clarify how error messages are displayed and how to address errors. It provides additional guidance on providing user-facing and localized error messages, including a specific recommendation to instruct users to interact with their phone if necessary. The changes improve clarity and provide more specific guidance on implementing error handling in media applications for cars.

entries.title: Support voice actions
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on integrating voice actions into Android Auto and Android Automotive OS (AAOS) media apps. The changes mainly focus on improving readability and clarity, including formatting code, adding a note, and refining the explanation of how voice actions are handled by apps.

entries.summary: This document introduces changes related to Android Auto app development. entries.title: Google Play on Android Automotive OS

The updated content now directly states that the provided table illustrates which Google APIs for Android are available on Android Automotive OS cars with Google built-in, and the table itself remains unchanged.

entries.title: Notifications on Android Automotive OS
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of how notifications function on Android Automotive OS. It includes updates regarding window insets, foreground service types, runtime-registered broadcast receivers, activity launching, the locale of CarContext, UI mode, component exporting, and pending intent mutability to ensure app compatibility and correct behavior on different Android versions.

entries.title: Designing for foldables

These features enable developers to manage UI elements and animations based on the state of the foldable device, such as the fold position or device orientation.

entries.title: MotionLayout reference
entries.summary: The document introduces MotionLayout and motion scenes. It clarifies that a motion scene file is an XML file that defines a motion sequence, referenced by the `app:layoutDescription` attribute in the layout file's `<MotionLayout>` node. This overview helps developers understand the core concepts and setup for using MotionLayout.

The documentation for `<Constraint>` element in MotionLayout has been updated. A video link has been added, and the documentation now explicitly states that the `<Constraint>` element supports standard ConstraintLayout attributes, directing users to the ConstraintLayout developer guide for details. This update provides direct access to video tutorials and clarifies the element's behavior.

The documentation for `ConstraintSet` has been updated with a link to a YouTube video and some minor formatting changes for clarity. Specifically, a video link was added and some lines were reformatted for better readability.

The documentation for `<KeyFrameSet>` has been updated to include a link to a video explaining how to use it. It also refines existing explanations for clarity and readability, clarifying how `<KeyFrameSet>` modifies the behavior of MotionLayout animations by using KeyPosition and KeyAttribute to specify intermediate states.

The documentation for `<KeyPosition>` has been updated to include a link to a video tutorial, improve the explanation of the element's purpose, and refine the descriptions of attributes. These changes enhance clarity and provide users with a better understanding of how to use `<KeyPosition>` to customize motion paths in MotionLayout.
The documentation for `motionscene.md` has been updated with minor formatting changes, clarifying language, and improved syntax. A video link has also been added at the beginning of the file.
The documentation for `<OnClick>` in MotionLayout has been updated. A video link has been added, and minor text refinements improve clarity, such as breaking up long sentences for better readability.

The documentation for `OnSwipe` has been updated with minor formatting improvements, and a link to a video tutorial has been added. These changes enhance readability and provide additional learning resources for developers.

The documentation for `<Transition>` in MotionLayout has been updated. A video link has been added, and minor adjustments were made to the text, including formatting and wording improvements for better clarity. The changes clarify how to specify start and end states within the transition definition.

entries.title: Accessing data using Room DAOs

entries.title: Write asynchronous DAO queries

It details how to create views using the `@DatabaseView` annotation, including an example, and how to associate them with the database through the `@Database` annotation's `views` property. These views allow encapsulating queries and are treated similarly to data objects within DAOs, enabling complex data retrieval operations.

entries.title: Define data using Room entities

entries.title: Referencing complex data using Room

It also reinforces the reason why Room doesn't allow object references, explaining the performance implications of lazy loading on the UI thread and suggesting the use of POJOs and joins for referencing multiple entities.
